Similarly, disco, funk and soul are the musical coordinates that Washington, D.C. native dreamcastmoe navigates. With a croon drenched in reverb, his songs manage to sound classic and hyper-modern at the same time. In his DJ sets, he plays a wide range of soulful tracks over which he sings live. Eliza Rose is a musician, singer and DJ from London who releases vocally powerful house tracks heavily influenced by the local soundsystem culture. Maryama Luccioni aka Maryisonacid is a DJ and founder of the label African Acid is the Future and the party of the same name in Berlin, weaving sounds of the African diaspora together with groovy electronic tracks and techno.

A self-proclaimed “selectress with no inhibitions”, New York-based DJ and producer Dee Diggs champions a return to the queer, Black roots of US house music. A love that was ironically sparked while studying abroad in France. Diggs' sets build on the rich heritage of disco, house, funk and soul, imbued with storytelling, straight-up lyrical tracks full of sensuality and refined grooves - as heard on her excellent FACT mix, for example, which weaves together tracks by MoMA Ready, Karizma, DJ Deeon, Solange and Parris Mitchell. Her just-released single “Toss It” merges disco extravagance with vintage house deepness and is, in our humble opinion, one of the best dancefloor numbers with pop appeal of the year so far. Dee Diggs will be playing at Panorama Bar as part of this Klubnacht alongside fellow DJs Kyle Hall and French Italo house aficionado Eliott Litrowski.

Besides its association with the tropical disease transmitted by mosquitoes, the word dengue is also slang in Peru with that special feeling of pre-party anticipation. Over the past ten years, the Berlin-based Peruvian duo Dengue Dengue Dengue have combined Afro-Peruvian music with modern electronics, now considered pioneers of a digital cumbia sound. In their sets and on their label Kebrada, they celebrate a wide range of cutting-edge music from Latin America. Ideal guest DJs for Darwin's Reef night, which is taking over both floors tonight. At Berghain, Hodge, Skeptical and SP:MC will focus on British bass music and dark garage tunes, while Dengue Dengue Dengue will be joined at Panorama Bar by New York-based Nigerian and organiser of the Groovy Groovy parties Akanbi as well as Berlin-New York duo Sha Ru.

FACT magazine called quest?onmarq one of New York's most fearless DJs, and once you listen to their genre-bending mixes, you know it's no exaggeration. Their sound is deeply rooted in the New York underground, bringing a full-on barrage of ballroom, techno, Jersey club, UK funky, grime and their own edits of pop songs into the mix. The first function they attended was Azealia Bank's Mermaid Ball in 2012, where they won a costume contest by wearing a vintage sweater adorned with shimmering sequins, taking home a $1,000 prize - money they invested in their first piece of audio gear. Their own productions have evolved from whip-cracking ballroom stompers to acid-soaked techno tracks, and we can't wait to see how they push Berghain to the limit for this Klubnacht. Besides peers such as such as JakoJako, Josey Rebelle and Kittin, two well-known DJs will be playing back-to-back for the first time: Luke Slater and Ø [Phase], together in the formation of Roog Unit.

French Berliner Emmanuel Corre aka E-Talking crashes onto Love On The Rocks with a 4-track EP, 'The Cosmic Egg' that’s weirder, faster and harder than anything Paramida’s ever-evolving imprint has put out to date; pushing the label into new territory that’s simultaneously unexpected and unmistakably true-to-form. It’s due out on September 14th. With his extremely punchy cuts, he's a perfect fit for Paramida's Love on the Rocks party, where he'll play today alongside Alex Kassian, Maayan Nidam and Paquita Gordon as well as Truly Madly: The London DJ has been active since the early '90s, but only in the last couple of years has an an international audience caught on to his smooth deep-house mixes.

*** Unfortunately, Luke Hess cannot play this weekend. Efdemin plays in his place*** As a former engineer and mathematician, Luke Hess has translated his original talents and determination into a creative context, as proven by his consistently high-quality output. With almost mathematical precision, the Detroit native has been producing perfectly imperfect-sounding dub techno tracks since the mid-2000s, always able to conjure up a moment of dancefloor meditation with expansive atmospheres, subtle modulations and forceful kicks. Minimalist, self-sufficient music in the best sense. His tracks have been released on labels like Omar-S' FXHE, Echocord and his own DeepLabs. For this weekend's Klubnacht , Luke Hess is DJing in Berghain alongside colleagues such as DVS1, JASSS and Lolsnake. Up at Panorama Bar, there will be a classic, feel-good lineup with the likes of Gerd Janson, Soundstream, AGY3NA and Virginia.

A pioneer of Detroit techno, Eddie Fowlkes boasts a discography of over 150 releases. Practically since his first EP was issued on Juan Atkins' Metroplex in 1986, he's been an integral part of the city's rich musical history. In the early 90s, after working closely with Berlin's budding techno scene surrounding Hardwax and Tresor, he cultivated a subgenre that he termed technosoul, characterized by its understatement and influenced by 70s funk, Motown and Chicago house. His latest productions, released in the form of two EPs each on Classic Music Company and Rekids, sound as fresh and compelling as ever with their swingy percussion, punchy drums and trippy vocals. Tonight, Fowlkes joins Rekids boss Radio Slave, Katerina and Monty Luke, also from Detroit, playing at Panorama Bar for the first time in ten years.

Páula, Povoa and Jerge are PPJ, a Parisian trio combining neoperrero, hard techno and electroclash-era pop. Their recent debut EP Bloco Vol. 1 features sugary sweet techno-pop songs like “Bicha” (Portuguese for “queer”) and gritty tracks fueled by Páula's vocals with freewheeling rhythms, Brazilian-style percussion and full-on whistle blasts. PPJ perform live at Säule for this weekend's edition of Klubnacht . Playing live at Berghain for the first time is Fireground, whose driving 90s techno features Detroit-schooled use of synths – a perfect fit for Tresor and Ilian Tape, the labels that released the Italian duo's most recent EPs. Ready in LED is the project of Ukrainian singer Olya Dibrova, who on her three EPs so far purveys a supremely engaging sound built on Italo-disco and dance-pop, catapulting you from a standing start into blissful arpeggio heaven – also playing live tonight at Panorama Bar.

Olof Dreijer recently released “Rosa Rugosa” via Hessle Audio, his first solo output in a number of years. With exuberant, hyper-emotional melodies that sound like a 2023 update of his previous releases as Oni Ayhun, he maintains his status as one of the most innovative producers to combine dancefloor sounds, pop and experimental music. As The Knife together with his sibling Karin aka Fever Ray, he paved the way for numerous queer acts, and since their disbandment in 2014, he has been focusing on solo dance releases and collaborations with other artists such as Tunisian multi-instrumentalist Houeida Hedfi, Swedish-Kurdish pop artist Zhala and, most recently, Mount Sims on a five-track album centered on the multi-faceted harmonies of the steelpan. For this edition of Finest Friday at Panorama Bar, Dreijer is joined by Natalie Robinson, Russel E. L. Butler and Suze Ijó.

Unfixed is the name of Barker's new EP, released in late August by Smalltown Supersound. Unlike his recent solo releases that have explored the possibilities of beatless techno, the tracks on his new EP are an impressive study in the variability of bass and kick drums. Raw, stuttering techno with an expressive sound design – just as you'd expect from Sam Barker. For this edition of Klubnacht , he will be playing at Berghain with the likes of Amsterdam-based DJ Mary Lake. As a Dekmantel resident, she too has a soft spot for unorthodox techno, electro and leftfield electronica, as heard on her appearances on EOS Radio, for example. In addition to Âme, Marie Montexier and Massimiliano Pagliara, Frankfurt's Luzie from the collective Mitte der Gesellschaft will be playing up at Panorama Bar.

Hardly any label brings 80s influences like EBM, industrial and dark wave into a contemporary techno aesthetic more coherently than the BITE label, co-run by Phase Fatale. For the label's fifth anniversary at Berghain and Panorama Bar, he'll be playing with label acts like Reka, Silent Servant (in a long overdue back2back with Phase Fatale), Unhuman and Vulkanski, resident of Tbilisi's KHIDI club, as well as friends and like-minded acts. Berlin-New York duo Enxin/Onyx, consisting of Tommi Tokyo and Nicky Mao, play dystopian ritual music infused with Mao's ghostly vocals, evoking the broken spirit of Throbbing Gristle. Meanwhile, Sharlese is considered one of the most exciting synth-wave and goth DJs on the American West Coast, serving as resident DJ of Seattle's underground techno collective secondnature and as a member of Black Techno Matters.

Mathew Jonson has blessed the techno world some of its most seductive, mysterious moments – it's not for nothing that his 2005 classic “Marionette” was once described as “SH101 snake-charming techno with a genius melody.” For the past two decades, the Berlin-based Canadian has been releasing music that fuses organic funk, elements of live jazz, harrowing basslines and the infinite expanses of sci-fi techno – whether solo or with his live improv ensemble Cobblestone Jazz. After releasing mostly collaborative EPs over the past decade, his latest effort, Into The 5D , is a solo record that shows why he remains one of the most high-profile producers around. Tonight, Jonson plays live at Säule for Klubnacht . There will also be DJ sets by Roman Flügel, house eccentric Axel Boman from Sweden, Zisko from Argentinia and Ruby Savage from London, where she throws the post-punk disco party In Flames , and many more.

Radiant Records is part of the expanded universe of the nomadic party series by Byron Yeates and his collective, Radiant Love. Across more than ten releases, a hedonistic prog-trance-ambient-house world unfolds in different tempos and styles, firmly anchored to the queer dancefloors of our time. French duo Baraka, consisting of singer and poet Hawa Sarita and DJ/producer Cristofeu, represents a hybrid dance-pop that brings together Sarita's catchy vocals with her partner's powerful percussion and acid sounds. In addition to their live gig, tonight's label party at Panorama Bar will feature DJ sets from Byron Yeates, Australian DJ Kia and Finnish electro producer Sansibar.

The Japanese concept of ma describes a temporal pause, an interval or a negative space, which can be applied to classical Japanese dance, architecture, a tea ceremony, communication or even music. The fact that Tokyo-based DJ MARIA. draws on this aesthetic philosophy that permeates all of life comes as no surprise. Her sets are hypnotic journeys with a floating, minimal techno sound that knows how to immediately transport you into a Zen-like state. Trance but not Trance. “Dance You Mutha”, the debut record by Mike Dunn released in 1987 on Bam Bam's Westbrook Records, anticipated everything to be heard from the Chicago DJ/producer in the subsequent years. With underground hits like “So Let It Be Houze!”, “Magic Feet” and “God Made Me Phunky”, he crafted an unmistakable hip-house style that's lost none of its sexiness to this day. Catch him at Panorama Bar for this Klubnacht .

Event: 2023-09-29 Finest Friday x mama told ya
PressText: Anetha's label and platform for emerging techno acts is taking over Berghain for tonight's Mama Told Ya party. Vel is a Moroccan artist living in France, not only a DJ/producer but also a trained engineer. Her music on labels like Francois X' XX LAB can be described as fast techno fueled by pulsating percussion, where her downright brutal drumming is countered with fragile voices and moods. Her live set will be complemented by DJ sets from Anetha, Nebuchadnezzar, Peachlyfe and France's Adrien Van de Velde aka A Strange Wedding. One floor up, the Finest Friday party features a wild musical ride spanning Hi-NRG UK rave sounds with Angel D'lite, the eclectic 90s-inspired style of Colombian DJ ISAbella, resident DJ of London's Homodrop party Marie Malarie, and the sibling duo S-candalo.

Dark Machine Funk is the name of the game for Irish-born, Manchester-based Kerrie, who runs the label that also provides an apt description of her sound. Mainly influenced by Detroit and UK techno of the 90s and 00s, she doesn't rely on increasing the tempo and noise level to reach her high energy, instead letting the tracks breathe and swing – a style that has earned her releases on labels such as James Ruskin's Blueprint. The former resident DJ of Manchester club Eastern Bloc will be playing at Berghain alongside colleagues such as Erika, Marco Shuttle, Robert Hood and Speedy J. CHERIII, originally from France, is the founder and resident DJ of the queer party series Homodrop , which took place at East London cult club Dalston Superstore before he made the move to Berlin five years ago. Through his label of the same name, he's released tracks such as “Labdance” and “Lockdown On Acid”, demonstrating his expertise on deviant dancefloors by bringing slow winding acid lines, Italo disco references and hi-NRG claps.

With great sadness Honey is going to miss "Jack Your Body" as she is attending the memorial of an immediate family member in Chicago. Honey has asked friend and collaborator Luke Solomon to step in for her. Spiritual, nasty, joy – these are a few of the words DJ Rimarkable has used to describe the style of her sets. With Puerto Rican roots and based in Detroit, she finds inspiration in Afro-Caribbean sounds alongside the distinctive musical influences of the city's techno legacy. Last year, she lent her voice to Honey Dijon, the hostess of Jack Your Body , for their collaborative single “Drama”, which was produced by British house legend and Classic Music Company owner Luke Solomon, who will be spinning at Panorama Bar tonight along with Rimarkable and another fantastic figure from Detroit, DJ Minx.

Mexican DJ Mystery Affair weaves together the loose ends of Italo disco, house and new-wavey dance tracks in her sets with an exceptional allure. Over the last ten-plus years, she has been a regular at the Sunday Sunday series in Guadalajara and queer spots throughout Mexico City, and with Monocero Records she runs a platform for “dirty disco, space techno, magic music”, whose seven releases to date form a feast for fans of these genres. Mystery Affair plays at at Panorama Bar for weekend's Klubnacht , alongside Ata, New Yorker Dee Diggs, Levon Vincent (who has just released his new album Sacred Geometry ) and Ohio-born Titonton Duvanté, who has developed an inimitably warm, intricate house style in his productions and sets since the late 90s. At Berghain, you can hear Berlin-based Spaniard Beatrice, who was recently appointed resident DJ at New York's Basement Club, and Madalba, one of the organisers and residents of the Buttons party.

Label party Live From Earth Klub is bringing together in-house artists such as DJ Gigola and Julian Muller with friends aligned with the Berliners' open sound philosophy. London-based Brit Effy offers a warehouse-ready sound, mixing acid-driven techno with more breaky sections – grime and dubstep have always been big influences on her. On her releases (whether solo or in collaboration with the likes of Mall Grab or Flowdan), an energy reminiscent of the Chemical Brothers' best moments shines through, such as on the track “Stone”. Meanwhile, you can expect temper-tantrum techno in the 160-bpm range by Mac Declos from France. After studying contemporary dance at the Centre Chorégraphique National of Roubaix, he discovered house music and by the age of 19 was already spinning alongside Laurent Garnier at the Astropolis festival.

Augmented Dreams is the new record by Alan Abrahams aka Portable, out 13 October on Circus Company. It's a concept album whose title refers to our everyday use of advanced technologies that allow us to realise past generations' fantastical visions today. It's all rendered once again with Abrahams' inimitable style of emotive songwriting laced with edgy breakbeats and Detroit-influenced, four-on-the-floor grooves, which he's sure to demonstrate in his live set at Säule tonight. Also playing live for this weekend's Klubnacht is Luke Slater with his Planetary Assault System at Berghain: techno hypnosis of the deepest variety with funky elements and Morse code messages from outer space. At Panorama Bar, you can hear Ceri Ann Owens, skilled in the art of nine-hour sets thanks to her stint London club Corsica Studios.

Red Laser Records was founded in 2012 in Manchester by DJ and party organiser Il Bosco, with a repertoire that perfectly at Italorama Bar with its Italo missiles, synth jizz sizzlers, analogue chuggers, boogie bombs, rave bangaz and acid heat. Also from Northwest England is today's live act Kid Machine, who, besides his releases on Red Laser, was picked up by Italo don I-F for his label Viewlexx, which put out his fantastic Replicants EP there in 2012: retro electro with a strong Italo disco influence. In turn, Australian DJ Hysteric is restoring lost and unjustly forgotten pearls of Italo history with his label Mothball Record, on which he has been releasing reissues as well as mixes and new material since 2011. Hysteric is playing tonight alongside Paty Vapor, Franz Scala and Shubostar.

Rave man, spiritual leader, mayor of enjoyment – that's how Lagos-raised, New York-based Akanbi describes himself. A healthy self-confidence that the DJ can back up as he deftly manoeuvring through all genres of dance music. In his mixes, for example for The Lot Radio, he glides through ethereal ambient, dub, Afrobeats and other low-frequency rollers, whereas at his own Groovy Groovy parties, he tends to harder sounds. This sort of sonic boundlessness is also embraced by fellow New Yorker DJ quest?onmarq. For their mix for Fact, the magazine called them one of the city's most fearless DJs, and it's easy to understand why when you join the ride through tracks by Jeff Mills, Sunn O))), Burial, M.I.A. and a number of their own edits. Also playing at Panorama Bar for Klubnacht is OMOLOKO from Brazil, who's behind the party series 101Ø in Bela Horizonte, playing everything from disco to world music, techno, house and broken beats.

***Due to a technical problem with the elevator, we are unfortunately unable to offer accessibility this weekend*** Since 2008, Bill Kouligas has been running PAN, one of the most exciting, innovative labels for electronic music, according to the general consensus. With a total of more than 140 releases, he has continuously shown an excellent sense of recognising talent (music as well as visual); he put out the debut records by many acts, building them up before they went on to gain international fame. Tonight's party at Berghain and Panorama Bar celebrates the label's 15th anniversary, paying tribute to the label's exceptional status with an equally exceptional lineup from the roster and beyond. Yves Tumor released his first album on PAN in 2017, then still a producer of predominantly psychedelic soundscapes and experimental noise, and has since become an art-rock cult figure whose latest album on Warp, Praise a Lord Who Chews but Which Does Not Consume; (Or Simply, Hot Between Worlds) , has been acclaimed by fans and press alike. Tumor plays at Panorama Bar, joined by the likes of Object and Kouligas himself. Berlin-based Finnish duo Amnesia Scanner perform their soundtrack to a ...

***Due to a technical problem with the elevator, we are unfortunately unable to offer accessibility this weekend*** Establishing herself as a resident DJ at lesbian nightclub Le Pulp in Paris, Chloé Thévenin co-founded the party and record label Kill The DJ in the early 00s. During the subsequent two decades, she has created an impressive oeuvre of dark, introspective music, influenced as much by shoegaze as minimal techno and electroacoustic compositions. On her own label Lumière Noire, she has more recently taken on a focus of releasing film soundtracks, but also albums that she recorded with the Bulgarian percussionist Vassilena Serafimova or the British guitarist Pete Harden. However, for this weekend's Klubnacht , she'll show that her heart still beats for the dance floor. Ezgi Hasçelik will also be playing, making her Panorama Bar debut. The classically trained violinist is the resident DJ and booking manager of Istanbul's oldest underground club, MiniMuzikhol.

Of all the defamatory charges lodged against Marie-Antoinette in the lead up to the French Revolution, one of the most outrageous was that she and her entourage would “play at being farmers” in Versailles. The newly-crowned queen was allowed an escape from the formalities of court life at her small chateau of Petit Trianon, which was gifted by her husband the King, along with dominion over its surrounding gardens. There, she would build an architectural folly imitating a rustic English hamlet that included a working dairy, a fisherman’s cottage, stables, a pig sty, a sheep pen, and a hen house. 3hd’s grand “Fools and Their Folly” club night at Berghain & Panorama Bar mirrors this rural idyll for entertaining aristocrats, in its equally exclusive and emancipatory space of musical, social, and physical experimentation—by order of the Queen. The closing event of Creamcake’s “Let Them Eat Cake” festival program creates its own Grand and Petit Trianon gardens for a decadent rave, celebrating the pleasure of a place where electronic, pop, and club sounds converge. As a semi-public gathering where otherness is honored in pursuit of self-determination and alternative realities, the “Fools ...

BEIGE's mixtape AMEN! Vol. 1, released on Eris Drew and Octo Octa's T4T LUV NRG label, has been described as a devotional hymn to the redemptive power of house music. The title has a double meaning: on one hand, it refers to the most-used drum sample in dance-music history, the amen break, and on the other, to the spiritual power inherent to subgenres such as gospel house. For this weekend's Klubnacht , the Detroit DJ/producer holds service at Panorama Bar, joined by the founder of the Black Artist Database (B.A.D.) NIKS as well as NYC duo The Carry Nation, who bring their city's nightlife traditions into the present with their lush, vocal-heavy sets. Playing at Berghain will be Belgium's Kr!z, who has been running the label Token for over 15 years as one of the most solid and productive platforms around for purist techno.

The November edition of Reef features, as usual, an expansive line-up that offers various permutations of UK bass, techno and breakbeats. Hailing from Dublin, EMA is a DJ, founder of the Woozy label and its eponymous party, booker at nightclub Yamamori Tengu in the Irish capital, Rinse FM resident and co-founder of the Skin&Blister collective, which promotes women, trans and non-binary creatives. Online culture, human overload, burnout and excessive consumerism are the topics Lee Gamble continues to explore musically. Released in late October, his new album Models reveals a kind of fusion between hyperpop and trip-hop, featuring ballads sung by cybernetic voices. Also present will be French DJ/producer Toma Kami, whose latest EP on Livity Sound has been described by Hard Wax as “outernational drumming-inspired, smart, trippy tech-house grooves”. In his sets, he mixes deep bass music, techno, jungle, electro and rap.

Panorama Bar from 23:59h, Berghain sunday from 18:00 h As the gentlemen are cavorting at Berghain and the Lab for FC Snax United, this weekend's Klubnacht starts at Panorama Bar, until the main floor opens for everyone on Sunday at 18:00 with DJ sets by JakoJako, Rødhåd and Tasha. The lineup upstairs includes Bristol DJ Danielle, who honed her decade- and genre-spanning craft at London record store Phonica, where she worked for more than ten years. Meanwhile, she passes on her skills and experience in Mix Nights, a non-profit organization she co-founded, which offers DJ workshops aimed primarily at women and non-binary people. And with DVS1 and Marcel Dettmann playing at Panorama Bar, it's a chance to experience the DJs in a rather atypically housey habitat.

For tonight's edition of their party Your Love , Lakuti and Tama Sumo are bringing along two artists who have been making club music history since early on. Li'l Louis grew up in Chicago and scored his first DJ gig at the age of 12, continuing through his teens playing soul and disco in his hometown clubs, before finding worldwide success in the late 80s with “French Kiss”. Outrageously raunchy and effective to this day, the house number set itself apart from the bulk of house and techno records of its day with a bold, mid-track slowdown and lusty moans from Chaka Khan backing vocalist Shawn Christopher. In 1992, after further underground hits like “Blackout” and “I Call You”, he released his second, wonderful, high-end, major-label soul album, Journey With The Lonely , largely ending his producing career thereafter. Maurice Fulton already made his mark on house music back when he co-produced Crystal Water's “Gypsy Woman (She's Homeless)”, further proving himself over the years through today by releasing some of the most idiosyncratic and extravagant tracks under countless pseudonyms and projects.

For The People is the name of the EP that Bulgarian producer KiNK released in spring on Hypercolour, and Hyper Epic followed on his own label Sofia. Neither of them could be better named, as they sum up the essence of the long-standing, extremely popular techno producer. You can't help but feel Strahil Velchev's relentless energy, love of musical adventure, grand gestures and unbridled enthusiasm – something that's reflected in his recorded music as well as in his constantly-evolving live sets. For this edition of Klubnacht , KiNK takes over Säule, turning it into a “Room to Jack”! Speaking of which: “Jack is the one that can bring nations and nations of all jackers together under one house” is one of the great, comforting adages that Chicago house gave us 35 years ago – one that still resonates in the sets of DJs like Eris Drew, Ogazón, Sedef Adasi and Virginia.

Laundrette is the party from David Elimelech, Partok and Roi Perez. For its first edition at Panorama Bar, they've invited Rotterdam deep-house DJ Suze Ijó as well as London duo Faff. The Big Dyke Energy and Dalston Superstore residents bring a sound that's like a never-ending fountain that bubbles with euphoria, where rough house beats and chunky basslines are topped with high-pitched vocals and blissful piano chords. Garage, house, techno and electro mixed with maximum rave appeal, humor and a pinch of nostalgia. Fittingly, the tracks they issue on labels like Hot Haus and Planet Euphorique bear titles such as “BJs On The Beach”, “Translucid Poppers Got Me Moving” and “Crappy Hardcore”.

Montreal duo Pelada combine raw, fast-paced techno beats with expressive screaming in Spanish, bringing politically-charged topics like social justice, violence against women and environmental destruction to the dancefloor. With an almost punkish energy, they've also been taking their songs to the stage since forming in 2014. Following the release of their debut album on PAN, they have just self-released their sophomore effort Ahora Más Que Nunca . Pelada will play live at Säule for this weekend's Klubnacht. It's been eight years since New York's Aurora Halal last performed a live set with us, back then at Panorama Bar. This time, she'll present her hypnotic, psychedelic techno sound live at Berghain for the first time. Upstairs will be Baby Rollén from the UK, playing here for the first time, a trippy house set b2b with his soulmate Gallegos.

Italians do it better – an old dance music adage that can be fully affirmed by two of the guest DJs at Paramida's Love On The Rocks party on the first Friday of December in the Panorama Bar. Giammarco Orsini is half of the duo Data Memory Access and releases solo on Heko Records, where he also does A&R work. His tracks are crystalline, sparkling acid tech-house in which influences from IDM, electro and early Italian house and trance records constantly pop up; as a DJ, he leans towards smooth transitions to keep the flow hypnotic. Also from Italy, Quest spices up his DJ sets with all kinds of obscure garage, techno and electro tracks, without ever losing the flow.

NYC Ballet-trained dancer, DJ and producer Giulia Fournier-Mercadante aka Gigi FM calls her work “gestural electronic music”. She perceives music mostly as movement and in different colours, which she combines in extremely hypnotic, deep techno sets. Already with her debut EP Magnetite , released last year on Bambe, she proved a certain artistic maturity: four tracks of sublime, transcendental techno tracks in the style of Sandwell District and the Italian hypno-techno maestri. For this weekend's Klubnacht , she will be playing with the likes of Clarisa Kimskii from New York. Deeply rooted in the US techno underground for over 20 years, she has been a resident DJ at the Brooklyn queer party series Merge since last year. Playing live up at Panorama Bar, Frenchman Sweely will set off his colorful fireworks of jazz, funk and pop-influenced house tracks alongside exceptional acts such as Actress as well as Sugar Free, who is his labelmate on Limousine Dream and Gene On Earth. And downstairs? That's where the coolest electro duo on earth, Miss Kittin & The Hacker, will perform their eternally seductive, new-wave tech poetry.

JADALAREIGN is not only a DJ but also the booker of the New York club Nowadays and the leader of a workshop series called Skillshare, in which she passes on her knowledge to other women of color as well as queer, non-binary and trans people. With this combination of musical skills and commitment to community work, she is a natural fit in the lineup of the Refuge Worldwide party at Panorama Bar. The Neukölln radio station has also invited London-based DJ, illustrator and comic artist Anu, who has a regular radio show on NTS and plays a wide-rangin mix of old Bollywood soundtracks, house and new-age in her sets. Coming from Bristol is Gramrcy, who runs the Peach Discs label along with Shanti Celeste and releases his own productions on labels such as Phantasy Sound and Hot Concept.

Location: Berghain; Panorama Bar; Elektroakustischer Salon; Säule
PressText: In their music, DECIUS take the unrepentant spirit of 70s New York bathhouses and 80s Chicago nightclubs and put it through a dirty South London filter, giving a contemporary edge to the sounds of disco and acid house. The project comprises the founders of Trashmouth Records and members of Paranoid London and Fat White Family, crafting darkly pulsating tracks that are essentially love letters to gay cruising culture, with titles like “Masculine Encounter”, “Look Like A Man” and “Quick Reliefs”. Naturally, they fit perfectly with the vibe we want for our club's 19th anniversary celebration – performing live at Säule. Once again this year, we are expanding our surface area and opening the Halle, which will become an ambient sound laboratory featuring acts such as Dasha Rush and Marco Shuttle, and of course we've assembled a jam-packed lineup for Berghain and Panorama Bar featuring new and old friends. Happy birthday to us!

HAMAM'a giren terler – Sedef Adasi welcomes you to the second edition of Hamam Nights at Panorama Bar, a sweaty party series created by our resident DJ as both a life-affirming homage to her Turkish-Albanian roots and a playground for queer culture. In addition to BASHKKA and Gabrielle Kwarteng, CEM will be playing at Panorama Bar for the first time – a good thematic fit as co-founder of the Herrensauna party. There will also be the Canadian duo MDSM aka Marine D. and Sophy M., whose friendship developed into a musical collaboration during the pandemic, birthing ethereal, acid trance sounds.

It's been exactly 20 years since Nathan Fake released his first single on James Holden's then fledgling label, Border Community. “Outhouse” laid the foundations for an extraordinary musical career: Picking up where early-90s UK electronica left off, he created beautifully meandering melodies, psychedelia orchestrated with a natural touch and hypnotic undertow, making a lasting impact on the 00s that hasn't lost any of its radiance even six albums later. File under: mushroom house. As an exclusively live act, he also set standards in terms of live techno improvisation, as you can experience tonight at Säule as part of our Klubnacht . In addition to a live act by modular magician Colin Benders, Reef residents Darwin and Esposito will take over the decks of Berghain, while Panorama Bar will be hosting acts such as Cinthie, Grace Sands and Seth Troxler as well as the American poet, singer, producer and resident DJ of Detroit's Temple Bar, Tammy Lakkis.

Hard-hitting power techno and nu rave meet wildstyle house and indie disco with Spandau20 at Berghain and Toy Tonics at Panorama Bar. In addition to label acts such as Anna Z. and trained composer and vinyl aficionado Dajusch, FJAAK will also be bringing along friends such as the Greek DJ/producer Anné. Her releases for labels such as Soma feature strong references to 90s Detroit and 00s techno. It's all hands on decks for the duo Funk Assault, aka Chlär and Alarico, who generally play extremely dynamic techno sets as a duo using six to eight CDJs and two mixing consoles. Up one floor, Toy Tonics founder Kapote will be joined by label acts such as the Venezuelan-born, Barcelona-based crate digger Gee Lane, who is influenced by the proto-house scene of the 80s and the Body & Soul nights of 90s New York. Louise Chen is behind the Girls Girls Girls parties in Paris, playing a wide range of fun music spanning disco, gospel house, early house and techno.

A sensual celebration with your chosen family at our Finest X-Mas party on Christmas Day, featuring Chris Cruse, French-born, Amsterdam-based Kléo and our almighty hosts of Your Love , Lakuti and Tama Sumo.

Location: Berghain; Panorama Bar; XXX-Floor; Elektroakustischer Salon
PressText: ***Unfortunately Virginia cannot play due to illness*** This year, our New Year's Eve party already kicks off on Saturday night at the usual Klubnacht starting time, unfolding as an extended version with more than 50 acts on all floors of the house. All the best for 2024!

DJ/producer Azu Tiwaline lives in the desert region of el-Djerid in southern Tunisia, where she combines field recordings from her surroundings and elements of traditional Berber music with stripped-down, hypnotic techno grooves. On her recently released second album The Fifth Dream , she takes a deep dive into a meditative trance, joined by French-Iranian percussionist Cinna Peyghamy on the tombak, an Iranian goblet drum. She also sees her DJ sets as a kind of research into trance states, largely integrating tracks that have tribal influences and mysterious soundscapes. We are looking forward to hearing Azu Tiwaline make her Berghain debut at Klubnacht alongside peers such as Inox Traxx, Paula Koski and Rødhåd. Making their premiere at Panorama Bar, meanwhile, is French DJ duo Abajour. The brothers organize parties in Paris, playing regularly in clubs such as Concrete, Rex and Machine du Moulin Rouge, where they combine a wide range of styles from techno and house to new beat, 90s trance and disco.

Cooking with Palms Trax is the name of the monthly two-hour radio show by Jay Donaldson, better known as Palms Trax, on NTS. Two years ago, he launched the label of the same name – CWPT for short – releasing mix tapes by DJs such as Budino and Tarzsa as well as wonderfully eclectic records that touch on early Chicago house, Italo disco, Afrobeat and dimmed-down, slow beats – in other words, the entire musical spectrum of his DJ sets. For this edition of our Live From Earth Klub series, DJ Gigola and Palms Trax will be joined by brotherly DJ duo ALCATRAZ, Berlin-based, MCR-T and Amsterdam's Young Marco at the Panorama Bar.

Reka Zalan is a Berlin-based DJ pursuing her passion for electronic music at various venues around the city. Over the past four years, she has been able to deepen her musical knowledge and hone her personal taste and style as co-organizer of the bi-monthly ://elements party at ://about blank, where she is also resident DJ and part of the booking team; as a member of the Coppi collective, a project space with weekly community-based studio sessions in an off-location at the border of Friedrichshain and Lichtenberg; and last but not least, as a staffer at legendary record store Hard Wax. For this weekend's Klubnacht , she will be playing the Berghain floor along with acts such as Detroit's Erika, Lolsnake and Ron Albrecht. As DJ and organizer of the Dig Deeper party series, Alison Swing has helped shape the last decade of downtown Los Angeles' underground dance scene. Now based in Berlin, she is playing at Panorama Bar for the first time.

The club program of CTM 2024 opens with a night at Berghain that takes a rather hypnotic and psychedelic direction. Robert Henke aka Monolake will test the possibilities of the new Berghain sound system with an immersive live set, a unique musical continuum defined by fractal beat crystals and endlessly echoing drones. On their new album V , Föllakzoid prove that the most minimal approach often provides the greatest space for transcendence. With their repetitive drum, guitar and synth sound, the band dives deep into realms where mind and body get lost in each other. Tonight's lineup for Panorama Bar was devised in collaboration with Skrillex, who, following his Berghain debut in the fall. On the Säule floor, Thai multidisciplinary artist Pisitakun will perform an iteration of his long-term artistic project “The Three Sound of Revolution”.

Liberté, égalité, solidarité – Spectrum Waves is a Parisian LGBTQ+ collective focusing on freedom, diversity and equality that regularly throws parties at the Gare des Mines, mailing featuring up-and-coming French and international FLINTA DJs, with part of the proceeds donated to charitable organizations. It was founded in 2017 by DJ Lea Occhi, meanwhile based in Berlin, whose sets are defined by groovy and fast-rolling techno with elements of tribal. At this edition of Klubnacht , Occhi will be playing at Berghain for the first time alongside cohorts such as Justine Perry, DJ Maria. and David Fogarty. One floor up, besides sets by New Yorker livwutang, Gabrielle Kwarteng and Dark Entries founder Josh Cheon, DJ Yes'in from Morocco will be making his return, mixing funk, disco, house and techno with organic grooves and a minimalist austerity.

Kicking off the night will be beatmaker Irish 40Hurtz , who is joined by the full-throated holler of wordsmith Hazey Haze , painting pictures of the peaks and troughs of Irish life by a distinct and proud Limerick accent. Armed with self-built controllers as they dive into and disrupt the crowd, the high-intensity live duo FITNESSS feat Lung combine into a suspenseful sonic assault of high-precision sound design and raw intense vocals, coursing with the distilled rage and sorrow of a decimated Los Angeles. Appearing with a live AV show crafted from his latest concept album Syntheism , experimental bass producer EPROM dives deep into ultra-digitalized soundscapes, chirurgic sound design and erratic showers of breakbeats, paying homage to acid, dubstep, drum'n'bass, breakcore, and many other genres, albeit with the always recognizable EPROM touch. Syntheism is titled after the belief of spiritual atheism, reflecting the producer’s wish to connect more deeply and meaningfully with other human beings. GASH Collective is a source of innovation on the more uncompromising strands of Irish underground music while focusing on supporting female, trans, queer, non-binary, and other ...

Appearing as the duo Soulsick , Elvin Brandhi and DJ Scotch Egg will join forces in a fierce flurry of improvised vocals and hardcore. Brandhi never shies from being abrasive, letting a stream of consciousness take over to the tune of frenetic, computerized beats intent on scorching your speakers. Berlin-based Japanese screwball producer Shigeru Ishihara aka Dj Scotch Egg (WaqWaq Kingdom, Scotch Rolex) has been active for over two decades ever since rising to breakcore (and Gameboy gabber) fame after co-founding Brighton’s Wrong Music with Shitmat, Roger Species, and others. A UK underground fixture and leading innovator in fresh genres spanning the hardtek scene, Mandidextrous will bring a diverse combination of high energy 4x4 dnb, speedbass, jungletek keeping true to their 4x4 175bpm style. High energy from start to finish. The sounds of Lobster Theremin and Never Sleep affiliate Infinity Division move through the world of dance music with blistering speed, fusing elements of industrial and electro, with a penchant for melancholic trance-infused melody, through a distinctly punk lens. Under his Special Request alias, UK techno & house linchpin Paul Woolford takes as his ...

Belgian techno producer Peter Van Hoesen is releasing his new album Towards the centre of time and surrounded by spirits in early March via Brussels label Vlek. Van Hoesen, currently living in Ho Chi Min, adds crystalline bleeps and obscure industrial sounds to his dense, purist techno. With ten years since his last album, his sound design remains more futuristic and otherworldly than almost anything else out there, and for this weekend's Klubnacht, he will present his work in a live set – the way he plays exclusively. One floor up sees a focus on US-American DJs with Daniel Bell, Magda and Mike Servito, who have each helped shape different decades of minimalist dance music. In the early 90s, Bell fused alarming 303s with the brutal stomp of European hardcore as half of Cybersonik along with Richie Hawtin; as DBX, he released completely skeletonized techno-funk tracks, creating one of the most morbid dancefloor hits of his generation 30 years ago with “Losing Control”. In the 00s, he pared down his sound even further and went in a housier direction, including projects such as the minimal supergroup Narod Niki, but Bell has remained true to his musical integrity to this day. This ...

Space Dimension Controller's output has always been an ode to a better future at the other end of the universe, with visual references to 70s retro-futurism and a wide musical range that paints a benevolent picture of a possible (dancefloor) world – an offering that has never seemed more valuable than today. Having released on such personality-driven labels as R&S, Ninja Tunes and Clone over the past 15 years, the Irishman's second acid-infused EP will be released in early February on Running Back, the label run by Gerd Janson, whom he says he met over a decade ago as an annoyingly enthusiastic, stoned 19-year-old obsessed with Break Machine and Paul Hardcastle. At this Running Back night, Space Dimension Controller will be playing live in the Panorama Bar, surrounded by an incredibly eclectic DJ line-up consisting of Finnish DJ and Running Back producer Katerina, Narciss, Gerd Janson and Berlin Duo DJ Heartstring.

Berlin-based Canadian Merlin Ettore is a pioneer of hybrid drumming, i.e. the combination of acoustic and electronic drums. This symbiosis enables him to create a complex, challenging sound in which gigantic beats meet heavily modulated timbres. In his Sorcery project, he plays with a purely electronic setup and releases on labels such as ara, run by Kangding Ray. For this weekend's Klubnacht, he will play live at Berghain for the first time. Two excellent DJs from Japan, Haruka and ¥ØU$UK€ ¥UK1MAT$U, will also be performing. For Years, Haruka has co-organized the Future Terror parties with DJ Nobu, with whom he also shares a smooth, seductive mixing style. ¥UK1MAT$U, on the other hand, is far from mellow: the Osaka-born DJ transforms his love of eclectic sounds into intense, adrenaline-soaked sets. Sepehr, a DJ, producer and label operator from the Bay Area and now based in New York, will be playing live at Säule on Sunday afternoon. His own music ranges from aphrodisiac, slow-moving acid house to psychedelic drum cocktails, and on his label Shaytoon he mainly releases electronic music by artists from West Asia.

With his releases on Toy Tonics, Sicilian artist Sam Ruffillo evokes the sound of the Italian Riviera and the hippie days of Ibiza in the late 70s. His take on smooth Balearic disco echoes his Italian disco predecessors: without the use of samples, he jams in his studio with guest musicians on guitar, bass, percussion and vocals – Italianissimo! Chloé Caillet, who grew up in New York, Paris and the UK, now resides in Ibiza, representing a new generation on the Spanish party island as Circo Loco resident DJ and founding member of the queer party collective XTRA. Her debut single “Love Ain't Over” became a hit in 2021 thanks to support from the likes of Gerd Janson, and she has curated music for the catwalk for Italian fashion house Miu Miu on several occasions. Along with London-based Guinny and the core team of Toy Tonics, Kapote and Stump Valley, Ruffillo and Caillet will be playing at Panorama Bar tonight.

London-based producer Ryan Lee West, better known as Rival Consoles, was the first act to be signed to the Erased Tapes record label 15 years ago. Since then, over the course of nine albums, he has been working on a kind of humanisation of electronic music equipment that makes his work sound organic and personal, similar to that of Jon Hopkins or Nils Frahm. In his debut appearance at Klubnacht , Rival Consoles will be presenting his captivating mix of ambient, electronic shoegaze and minimal techno live at Säule. Also making her Berghain debut is Spanish DJ Anika Kunst, who releases her jagged, chord-driven techno on Ben Sims' Symbolism and is a resident at the Macarena Club in Barcelona. Brooklyn-based DJ/producer Antenes' sets range from acid-heavy techno to shimmering electro and dark ambient sounds. Her productions, created on self-built sequencers and modular synthesizers, have been released on L.I.E.S. and the Canadian ambient label Silent Season.

Another edition of the Your Love party series, lovingly curated by Lakuti and Tama Sumo, this time presenting two guest DJs from the USA whose praise cannot be overstated. New Jersey-born DJ Qu once described himself as “the dancer's DJ”, which is not only a reflection of his DJ skills but also refers to his background as a world-traveling house dancer in the 90s and as a former resident at the New York HouseDanceConference. DJ Qu has also been producing for the past two decades, releasing mainly on his own label Strength Music, creating his own dark voodoo house sound. Mark Grusane, known in Chicago as the former co-owner of the record store Mr. Peabody Records, has a profound knowledge of dance music history reaching far beyond his own city. He is particularly fond of the sounds of the mid-70s through mid-80s, having released three excellent compilations of disco, soul and funk rarities from this era on BBE. His own music, which he releases on labels such as Rush Hour or his own Disctechno, is old-school Chicago house at its best – jack your ass off!

Gaston Volpe is an Argentinian DJ/producer whose music is heavily influenced by the 90s sound of the Detroit-Berlin axis. His recently launched label Transcent has released The Soul Is Healed EP, a superb EP which that doesn’t shy away from the great dub techno tracks of that era, with its massive reverb, engaging chords and syncopated percussion. He created all its tracks in one take by jamming, a good indicator for his live gig at Berghain for this weekend’s edition of Klubnacht. Berlin-based Lars Huismann and Amsterdam-based Rosati, two acts with current releases on Steffi's label Dolly, will also be playing. Rosati describes his style as ”hand-crafted“, always offering a human touch in his work, be it techno or house. Kenny Dope from New York is one of the most important house DJ/producers, with many accomplishments including sharing the US dance sound of the 90s. The son of Puerto Rican immigrants, he has released countless successful house remixes, whether solo or together with Little Louie Vega as Masters At Work, has been nominated for a Grammy four times, and is a gifted DJ and living encyclopaedia of beats – supa dope at Panorama Bar.

Kreuzberg record store institution Sound Metaphors is setting up shop at Panorama Bar, bringing along a few special guests. Acting Press is the fantastic vinyl-only label run by PLO Man and C3D-E, releasing carefully curated and usually quickly sold-out records that sound like the evolution of early-90s ambient techno. C3D-E will play a live set of his dancefloor-ready stoner techno, joined by Alicia Carrera, curator of the Club Cosmic series at Sameheads in Neukölln; Carl H, founder of the Animals on Psychedelics label; Donato Dozzy, Italian maestro of minimalist hypnotic techno; Ece Özel, DJ/promoter from Istanbul; and of course, a tandem set by Sound Metaphors heads Nemo and Castro. Bang!

David Letellier aka Kangding Ray has undergone a fascinating musical development, from his beginnings in ambient music to an unmistakable techno style marked by precision and structure, woven into a crystal-clear sci-fi aesthetic. In his almost architectural music, the deepest of bass tones, intricate rhythms and wondrous melodies merge with abstract sound design, as you can experience tonight in his live set at Berghain. Rebecca Goldberg, a member of the Detroit Underground collective, has released music and multimedia projects ranging from acid techno to experimental electronic music, and this weekend, the 313 Acid Queen is playing at Klubnacht for the first time. AIDA, who began her career in the Vancouver rave scene before moving to San Francisco, is one of the acts playing at Panorama Bar. Her sound has evolved from minimal to electro, breaks and progressive house. In addition to playing clubs, she runs Apranik Records, a record label with roots in Iranian activism that pushes artists of Iranian descent.

With a focus on house music by FLINTA acts, the second annual 1. FC GRRRLS UNITED on International Women's Day moves to Panorama Bar. Adrian Blount aka GodXXX Noirphiles is an American non-binary artist and parent of the drag and performance art collective House of Living Colors, exploring themes such as Afrofuturism, feminism, sexuality and non-binary identity. Tonight, Adrian will present a vocal performance next to several DJs who put their own spin on house music. Along with a few newcomers making their Berghain debut, Natalie Robinson is back again, weaving funk, soul, R&B, garage and traditional Ghanaian sounds into her set. The profits of this night will be donated in equal parts to Hydra e.V. ( https://www.hydra-berlin.de ) and LesMigras ( https://lesmigras.de ).

Batu from Bristol has been shaping the British techno sound since 2014 with his label Timedance. As a producer and with his radio show A Long Strange Dream, he tends to explore slower, more experimental realms, whereas in his DJ sets, he fittingly focuses more on the dancefloor, presenting various global club sounds and shifting tempos, from drum and bass to techno. For this weekend's Klubnacht , he will be playing at Panorama Bar with fellow DJs whose brand of house is so deep, it's below sea level. In the late 80s, Chez Damier was crucial in founding the legendary club The Music Institute in Detroit. He did A&R work for KMS and co-founded Prescription with Ron Trent, the groundbreaking Chicago label that released iconic deep-house tracks such as “Morning Factory” in the mid-90s. Hailing from France, DJ Deep is a proponent of classic techno and house sounds, with his finger on the pulse of Chicago, Detroit and New York, whether in his club sets, on his label Deeply Rooted or in his radio shows.

Polari Records is a hi-NRG house label run by Cormac. The name refers to the slang of gay subculture in Britain until the legalization of homosexual acts in the late 60s – a constantly evolving mixture of Italian, English, circus jargon, Yiddish and Romani. The Polari label night at Panorama Bar welcomes Josh Caffe, a super-charismatic live act who has also made a name for himself as a DJ, producer and vocalist – be it with his solo productions, as frontman of the acid house group Paranoid London or as a featured guest with Honey Dijon. Visiting from France, Jordan Nocturne – relatively new to the label with the Dolly EP (its title means “pretty” in Polari) – has been DJing since the age of 13 and has released on Correspondant, Permanent Vacation and his own label Nocturne. There will also be DJ sets from Chinaski, fka.m4a, Glasgow duo LEZZER QUEST and Romy. Heartcore vibes!

Verraco, DJ/producer from Medellín, Colombia, is known for his fusion of Latin American tribal techno, hyper-electronica and modern perreo bass. He co-runs TraTraTrax and Insurgentes, both significant platforms for Latinx producers. “Escándaloo” – the title frack from his first EP for Blawan and Pariah's label, Voam – was a festival rave hit last summer with its rambunctious kicks and escalating sirens. He'll be playing at this edition of Klubnacht along with the likes of Isabel Soto from Venezuela: Over the past couple of years, the Montreal-based DJ and founder of NYXII Records and member of the Arder collective has released her first tracks on labels such as Soma and Second State. Wallace, who lives in the tranquil medieval town of Shrewsbury in the West Midlands, has organized long club nights in Edinburgh and is now fully focused on furthering his skills as a DJ and producer. Wallace's debut album Red, Yellow, Black was released last year on Mule Musiq, merging nature sounds and field recordings with an emotionally charged house sound.

Although Cyan85 started his career just a few years ago, the Leipzig-based DJ has already established himself as a serious expert in Detroit-influenced electro with a steady flow of releases. His tracks on labels such as VOITAX and Curtis Electronix combine influences from Miami bass, ghettotech, booty house and acid to create a contemporary take on the tradition of Model 500 and Dopplereffekt. Partiboi69 has his very own approach to the genre: the Australian hedonist has created a musical and visual universe in which he reigns as a DJ, martial arts expert, rapper, fashion guru and sex icon. On his EP for Gerd Janson's Running Back label, out this month, he shows his sensitive side for the first time. With a background in performance art, x3butterfly is a Mexican-American DJ whose sets are inspired by early industrial and techno pioneers such as Throbbing Gristle and Robert Hood. Filling out the the lineup is Italian Berliner DJ Younger Than Me just released his debut album The Golden Age of Love on his label 90s Wax – a dynamic mix of progressive house, trance, EBM, breakbeat and techno elements.

A piece of advice that Love On The Rocks label boss PARAMIDA has given to artists sending her demos over the years: “Imagine it's 1992. You're at a rave on the beach in Goa. It's 8 in the morning. What track is playing? Make that track!“ Back then, all sorts of things were being played in Goa: techno, Italian house, new beat and post-punk, a fusion of different genres that would only later develop into what we now call Goa trance. And this still untamed, unpolished mixture of psychedelic, uplifting, vibrating dance music is the tradition that her label stands for, which can be experienced in the form of several back-to-back sets for tonight's showcase at Panorama Bar. You can also look forward to Andy Butler aka Hercules & Love Affair, whose selections range from proto-house and the golden era of the 90s in New York to Chicago Jack tracks and contemporary house. There will also be a live act at Berghain: Austrian techno producer and sound-design lecturer Arthur Robert will demonstrate his love of Detroit-inspired grooves.

Sedef Adasi is taking over Panorama Bar for the third time with her Hamam Nights series. This edition of the spa-themed rave features two back-to-back sets. Parisian DJ 131bpm has built up his reputation playing at queer parties, with sets influenced by various subgenres of electronic music such as vocal house, Detroit classic electro breaks, 90s progressive and Latin music. Tonight, he'll share the decks with Coco Cobra, a member of soft spot, a FLINTA/queer DJ collective based in Hanover. The second tandem set comes from Sedef and the label owner of Planet Euphorique, D. Tiffany from Canada. London-based Iranian producer Kasra V, who's had a radio show on NTS for the last ten years and has released on labels such as Radiant Love and Shaytoon, will once again show a strong passion for the breaky side of house music.

Berghain starts Sunday 4pm after SNAX Club. As always, Klubnacht on the long Easter weekend starts at Panorama Bar, and this time there are some special treats such as a special house set by DJ Rush. The Chicago legend played his earliest gigs back in the late 80s at the legendary Music Institute before developing his very own, sexually-charged spin on house in the early 90s, releasing on labels such as Trax, Dance Mania, Relief and Djax-Up. Even though Rush has been synonymous with a very hard and fast techno sound for the last 20 years, his roots are in disco and Chicago house, as you can hear this weekend. On Sunday at 16:00, once SNAX Club shuts down, we will open the door to Berghain. Besides DJs such as Honey Dijon, Dax J and Lolsnake, ItaloJohnson will play a farewell set, with a double meaning: after 15 years together, the Berlin trio will officially disband and play their very last set to close out Panorama Bar. Ciao e grazie per la musica!

Over the past three years, Berlin-based Australian producer Jennifer Loveless has put out five releases on labels such as Butter Sessions and Sex Tags UFO, emitting good vibes with her kaleidoscopic house sound. As resident DJ and curator of queer parties such as HOMODROP and Happy Endings at Dalston Superstore, East Londoner Marie Malarie supports up-and-coming artists. S-candalo is a DJ duo consisting of siblings Tania and Dominik Humeres-Correa (THC & DHC), who grew up in Germany with Colombian and Chilean roots. Showing an old-school 90s influence, their sound is imbued with queerness and energy, something they also cultivate in their own party series La Noche. Irish DJ/producer Sally C, who also lives in Berlin, has made a name for herself through a captivating mix of vintage house, acid, breakbeats and rave-inspired tracks.

Slam are a Scottish techno institution that has witnessed all the developments in electronic music across more than three decades. Stuart McMillan and Orde Meikle began their career in Glasgow, making their first appearance at the influential Sub Club in 1989. The duo's breakthrough came in 1993 with the classic “Positive Education”, followed by the release of Daft Punk's first records, establishing their label Soma as one of the top sources of dance music in Europe. Even now, over 600 releases later, the label is still going strong, with the current Activate EP as a case in point: raw, mental techno hypnosis at the highest level. Slam will be playing live in Berghain for this edition of Klubnacht . Another pioneer in his field is Chida, who has been shaping the underground house scene in Tokyo since the early 90s. Known for his marathon sets often lasting more than twelve hours, he was a regular player at Cocktail d'Amore in Griessmühle, and from 2009 to 2019 also released Balearic house tunes by like-minded producers from Tokyo, London, Stockholm and Berlin on his finely curated label, Ene.

In 2015, James Creed and Josh Tweek from the UK founded The Ghost in Berlin, a second-hand record store in a Mercedes camper van; the name was meant to symbolize the transience of the shop on wheels. With a carefully selected vinyl collection and the growing popularity of vinyl culture at the time, they attracted plenty of customers. As resident DJs under the same Moniker at a Berlin club floating on the Spree, they surrounded themselves with DJ friends such as Gene On Earth, KRN and Sugar Free. In 2022, they closed the store, sold the van and focused on their new label, Ghost Recs. For this edition of Gene On Earth's Limousine Dream night, some of the crew are reuniting, and cruising along is British producer Ron Obvious with a live set and London-based Canadian Peach, whose DJ sets are known for their warm, friendly and funky vibes.

Colin Benders has had a notably unusual musical trajectory: a former jazz and hip-hop trumpeter who enjoyed crossover success as bandleader of a 50-piece orchestra before discovering his passion for modular synthesizers and live techno jams. For this edition of Klubnacht , he will play an exclusive live set at Berghain together with Steve Rachmad aka Sterac, completely improvised with an impressive arsenal of hardware. This is only their second performance together after debuting at last year's Fusion festival. Playing at the Panorama Bar for the first time is Victor. Born in Berlin in the 90s, he immersed himself in the world of electronic music at an early age and started DJing in the city's clubs at age 16. He now has residencies at Munich's Blitz Club and festivals Lighthouse and Nachtdigital, and with his own record label Magic Power has four releases to his name.

Berlin-born Polish DJ and producer Charlie (no relation to “Spacer Woman”) is an active member of Krakow's underground dance scene. She is known for her event series SZUM and serves as co-director of the project space Elementarz Dla Mieszkańców Miast, recognized as one of the most influential art initiatives in the city. Her debut EP Cold Inside , inspired by synth-wave and Italo disco, was released on Ferry Lane Records in 2022. For tonight's edition of Italorama Bar x Slow Motion , Charlie will be playing live – complemented by DJ sets from artists on the Italian-German label, which has been championing the rich heritage of Italian dance music since 2009. For example, Marcello Giordani is appearing under his new alias Italo Deviance: Inspired by early NYC disco and the Chicago house scene as well as Italo and cosmic sounds of the early 80s, Giordani founded the blog Italo Deviance and a label of the same name in 2008. His prior Italo disco-influenced productions, released under his birth name, have appeared on labels such as Mule Musiq, Endless Flight and of course Slow Motion.

This is the Panorama Bar debut for Berlin-based DJ Yamour. Over the past few years, the vinyl lover has developed a sound that is strongly oriented towards the roots of American techno and house, which she was able to refine and deepen during her four years at the Hard Wax quality cadre factory. Hailing from the Netherlands, Frits Wentik will also be playing here for the first time. As part of the duo Will & Ink and owner of the record label of the same name, he initially pursued a boundary-pushing concept concept of techno, only to focus in recent years on house tracks characterized by raw drums, analog bass and wild, lo-fi samples. His experimental spoken-word album Safe Passage, which he recorded together with photographer Erik Madigan Heck and actor Tilda Swinton, was released three years ago on Dekmantel. Furthermore, two of the best live electronic acts of their genre will be performing on Sunday afternoon: EBM expert Terence Fixmer and minimal mastermind Mathew Jonson.

As usual, this edition of REEF dives deep with a sonic selection ranging from techno, drum'n'bass and house to South African dance music styles such as amapiano and Afrotech. A royal rep of gqom is DJ Lag, who taught himself to DJ and produce as a teenager in Durban. As the charismatic figurehead of a vibrantly creative regional scene, he is not only responsible for some of its most powerful and catchy tracks, but has also confidently pushed the boundaries of a subculture with his collaboration with Beyoncé and his cross-genre debut album, Meeting With The King . Bristol-based Neffa-T has made a name for himself with his technical ability in mixing tracks. His skillful sets, mixed on four to six decks, include dubstep, techno, grime and more. The Belfast-born painter, multi-instrumentalist and DJ Calibre has now released 24 albums, which are just as diverse as his DJ sets, ranging from drum 'n' bass to dub, techno, house, ambient, jazz, soul, blues and folk. Born in Mauritius, GЯEG is a resident of the Parisian party Le Creole and mixes jungle, techno and house with sounds from the African diaspora.

A real treat for anyone who's enthusiastic about the roots of American house music: As resident DJ at Newark club The Zanzibar since 1982, Tony Humphries developed what became known as the Jersey sound, deep house with a spiritual, soulful edge as it combined influences from gospel, R&B, jazz and danceable new wave after the end of the disco era. Humphries is considered one of the most pioneering DJs of this era and has been working on his cross-cultural style ever since. Another generation, similar influences: New York duo musclecars have been hosting their party Colouring Lessons in Brooklyn since 2018, and their sets as well as their recent releases on BBE are influenced by Afro-Latin percussion, jazz, soul, acid and classic house, all with a modern touch. Boo Williams and Mark Grusane can be described as pioneers of Chicago house. Williams' DJ career, like Grusane's, began back in the early 80s, and as a producer of raw, house-funk tracks on classic labels such as Relief, he was ultimately seen as representing the second wave of Chicago house in the mid 90s. Besides these several unmissable DJ sets, on Sunday afternoon, Portable will be playing live in celebration of his new ...

On the eve of May Day, a Finest Tuesday party with house-oriented sets by two otherwise techno-leaning mainstays, Amotik and Ben Klock. Joining them is Natalie Robinson, who grew up in a multicultural household and early on developed a deep connection to gospel, funk, soul, R&B and traditional Ghanaian music – the roots that form the basis of her lively DJ style.

For the second edition of their party Laundrette, its hosts David Elimelech, Partok and Roi Perez have invited Shay Malt, the creator of the London party series Adonis, whose hedonistic, chaotic and friendly vibe has been credited for a renaissance of queer underground parties in the city. One of the Adonis resident DJs is Grace Sands, who will also be spinning at the Panorama Bar tonight. A pioneer of the British soundsystem scene, Sands was a co-founder of DiY Soundsystem, a free-party collective from Nottingham that was committed to inclusivity and freedom since originating in 1989. From Queens, New York, comes Kilopatrah Jones, whose house mixes for the likes of Honcho and Colouring Lessons provide a strong dose of serotonin with a pinch of old Sound Factory grandeur.

Inspired by club sounds from Detroit, Berlin and the UK as well as post-punk, wave, EBM and electro, Phara creates techno tracks with pumping energy and drum sequences. His releases can be found on labels such as Soma, Planet Rhythm and his own label Phaar. The long-time Brussels Fuse club resident and vinyl DJ has been playing live shows for two years, where he uses a minimalist set-up, using the Korg EMX-1 groovebox, to achieve maximum impact on the dancefloor – live tonight at Berghain. Detroit's Luke Hess has been a reliable source of serious, dub-influenced techno for at least 15 years. His records on labels such as Echocord, FXHE, Planet E and his own DeepLabs testify to a deep understanding of deep electronic music and its technical execution – his background in engineering probably comes in handy here. After he had to cancel his scheduled gig at Berghain last August, we are delighted to be able to hear him this time at Klubnacht – alongside DJs such as Steffi and Radio Slave, on whose labels Hess has also released in recent years.

Jack, jack, ja jack jack your body with Miss Honey Dijon and her two guest DJs. Based in Berlin, Cody Curry is a musician from London whose idiosyncratic house sound is influenced by jazz and disco. Cody studied music in his home city, where he played jazz piano. He creates tracks that combine the knowledge of a trained musician with the skills of a producer, using only live instruments and no samples. After releases on labels such as Razor-N-Tape and Classic, his debut album Lucas was released on Toy Tonics in 2022. That same year, South African artist Desiree released her first EP, Femme Tech , on Jackie Queen's label Bae Electronica. The self-described “intersexy African interpreter of dance music” is in her mid-twenties and considers herself an activist for the queer community and POC in an industry still dominated by men, having co-founded a female-led electronic music collective called Boys Club.

As we all know, house is not just a feeling, but also healing. For this edition of Klubnacht, Aurora Halal will be playing up at Panorama Bar, having swapped stations with Luigi Di Venere, who will be playing a techno set down on the big floor. That's also where London-based DJ/producer Jin Synth will be taking over with hypnotic soundscapes that skillfully incorporate various analogue noise elements and ambient sounds recorded with special microphones.

On the International Day Against Homophobia, Transphobia and Biphobia, the queer series HOMODROP is hosting an edition of their party at Panorama Bar for the first time. Exactly ten years ago, French DJ CHERIII founded his own club night with resident DJs such as Victor Kubin at the renowned Dalston Superstore in East London, to which they have remained true to this day (besides additional events in Berlin). That's also the venue where London DJ Michelle Manetti organizes her bi-monthly queer-feminist party Fèmmme Fraîche. They are bringing along Kiddy Smile, one of the most iconic DJs on the French ballroom scene. The singer, dancer and producer is influenced by the formative house sounds of the 80s and 90s; one of his last EPs was called Paris' Burning Vol. 1 , in reference to Jennie Livingston's cult documentary. Furthermore, Verity Mayes aka Maze, resident DJ of the international party series HE.SHE.THEY, will be playing disco, house and melodic dance tracks.

Jamie Watson aka Jamie 3:26 grew up in the heyday of the Chicago music scene, when house was still an undefined fusion of new wave, punk, EBM, funk and soul, as defined by DJs such as Ron Hardy and Lil Louis. As a DJ/producer of the so-called second wave, Watson continues this tradition, as you can hear from his regularly released disco edits. In 2020, he released the compilation A Taste Of Chicago on BBE, on which he re-edited Windy City classics by Jungle Wonz and Chip E. That year, he also started the series Dancefloor Damage , issuing exclusives from his personal archive, having released its 24th volume last month. For this weekend's Klubnacht, Jamie 3:26 will be playing at Panorama Bar, where KC Wray, a Chicago peer with a similar musical education, will also be performing for the first time. Also premiering will be Brit Jayson Wynters, who began his career as a garage MC in Birmingham before Mr G discovered him as a solid producer of cosmic techno tracks and debuting him on his label Phoenix G. in 2016.

Dystopian cyberpunk aesthetics meet chilled synthwave, masculine EBM, Italo and techno – since 2018, Hayden Payne aka Phase Fatale has been running BITE as a platform for musicians who continue the legacy of the 80s with the production methods of today. For the BITE Night on both floors, Payne will be DJing with Pablo Bozzi under their Italo body music alias Soft Crash along with label acts such as Unhuman as well as DJ friends such as The Hacker. Active since the early 00s, Schwefelgelb is a Berlin techno duo who released a classic on the label aufnahme + wiedergabe in 2017 with their Dahinter Das Gesich t EP. Their energetic live sets are fueled by storms of mind-altering strobelight. Also performing live is Colombian producer Faunes Efe aka Filmmaker, who has released his raw post-punk-meets-EBM tracks on labels such as Opal Tapes and Veyl.

Over the past 17 years, Elisa Bee has made a name for herself as a record collector, producer and DJ. She began on her home island of Sardinia and since 2012 has been releasing her own music, most recently on labels such as Truncate, Balkan Vinyl and I Love Acid. She regularly plays all-night sets at her resident club Tempio Del Futuro in Milan, which she describes as a musical journey: “acid, very groovy, also dark sometimes, energetic, dreamy at the end”. For this edition of Klubnacht, Elisa will be playing on the main Berghain floor, joined by her Berlin colleague Ignez, founder of the Somov Records label and resident DJ at Eerste Communie Events. His hypnotic, pounding techno sound is inspired by the endless waves and long dunes of the Dutch coast. He shares his affinity to nature with Rødhåd, with whom he has released two collab EPs on his label WSNWG. In February, he released his debut album Tides on Token , continuing the nature theme through expressive sound design.

DJ Gabrielle Kwarteng's enthralling style of mixing combines NY house, techno, disco and percussive elements to create a dynamic atmosphere on the dancefloor. Born in Brooklyn to Ghanaian parents and raised in the Bronx, she was surrounded by Afrobeat and disco growing up. While studying humanities at a university in the state of New York, she made her foray as a host on college radio. In 2017, Kwarteng's show for The Lot earned her a Mixcloud Online Radio Award for “Best Eclectic Show” – starting her DJ career. Tonight she joins Roman Flügel and Ryan Elliot for the Finest Friday party at Panorama Bar.

***Len Faki unfortunately has to cancel, Altinbas will fill in*** Ina Kacz's musical identity spans a broad spectrum of electronic genres, with techno as the connecting thread. The French-Polish DJ shuttles between the two techno capitals of Berlin and Tbilisi, representing a deep, texturally rich sound with hypnotic rhythms. She released the Endless EP in late April on her own new label, Aneelhi. Following her ambient set at Halle am Berghain on New Year's Eve, she will be making her debut on the big floor at this weekend's Klubnacht. Another first-timer at this edition is Sydney-based Australian Deepa, founding member of the South Asian creative collective Kerfew. Back when she was a member of the DJ duo Honey Point, she mainly played jazzy deep house and neo-soul, since then has developed a warm, grungy dance sound that ranges from deep house and Jersey club edits to electro and breaks.

Alex Kassian is a Kyoto-born, Berlin-based DJ/producer whose musical style is heavily influenced by the spaced-out euphoria of early-to-mid-90s trance. In his tracks for labels such as Test Pressing and Love On The Rocks, he fuses holistic, swirling ambience with MDMA-evoking trance synths and progressive house elements to create a sound drenched in light, suited for the dancefloor and beyond. Kassian will be playing live at the Love On The Rocks party. Christian AB, a British DJ/producer also living Berlin, focuses mainly on the medium of vinyl, releasing on the likes of Timeless, the label of Francesco Del Garda. LOTR founder PARAMIDA will be joined by Cromby from Ireland and Zozo from Turkey, the latter of whom is resident DJ at Sameheads in Neukölln and member of the Zurich-based electronic music and art collective Les Belles de Nuit, which supports women and queer artists.

The music scene in Tokyo is a haven for tech nerds, marked by an extremely high level of quality, from listening bars and well-stocked record shops to high-end equipment in clubs, including specialized DJ cartridges and soundsystems. Perhaps less well known is that there are also sex-positive, queer parties. Since 2020, Mari Sakurai has been co-organizing the queer open-air party SLICK, regularly collaborating with party collectives from Berlin. Her DJ style is defined by fast, powerful techno, but she's not afraid to incorporate experimental ambient tracks into her sets. For this edition of Klubnacht, she will be making her Berghain debut. Charles Moon, who lives in Los Angeles and Mexico City, will be playing at Panorama Bar for the first time. As a sound engineer, he was nominated for a Grammy for his work on the hip-hop album Big Baby DRAM , and as a DJ, he plays extremely deep US house tracks and recently founded his own label, Thank You For Sweating.

Swiss DJ/producer Audrey Danza is the driving force behind Proxima, a record label that, in collaboration with Kreuzberg record store Sound Metaphors, reissues trance records from the 80s and 90s as well as new tracks by acts such as Kasper Marott, Budino and Danza herself. Do The Taboo is a record label and party series run by Cologne-based DJ, poet and producer Bryan Kessler, whose tracks have also appeared on Innervisions and Cómeme. DJ City is a DJ/producer with a penchant for the early years of house music whose Italo disco and synth pop-inspired tracks have been released on Public Possession over the past several years. The fourth figure in the lineup for tonight's edition of Finest Friday is Pablo Bozzy with his industrial body music.

Venezuelan DJ Leeon, based in Bogotá, is one of the key figures in the Colombian house and techno scene. Besides DJing, he helped established the local Video Club, was part of the Ediciones Danza Negra label collective, and has booked none other than Panorama residents Tama Sumo and Lakuti for the first edition of his new queer party series, Ritmo Caliente. Leeon not only plays bass-heavy, psychedelic techno but, as proven in his Phonica podcast, can also drop fabulous deep house sets. House, acid and noisy techno with melancholic undertones are the musical coordinates of Johannes Paluka, who since releasing his 2008 debut EP on Mirau has continued to putting out excellent music on labels such as Smallville, Acid Test and Frank Music – whether alone under his alias Iron Curtis, as part of the duo Achterbahn D'Amour or in collaboration with Johannes Albert on their album trilogy Moon .

Musclecars is a duo from New York consisting of Brendan Weems and Craig Handfield, who focus heavily on house, disco and soul, harking back to the golden age of the dance music scene in their home city – one of the best things to happen to the Big Apple's club landscape in recent years. That's where they've been hosting the Coloring Lessons party series, founded in 2018 and followed by the eponymous label two years later. Having already played at Lakuti and Tama Sumo's party at Panorama Bar, this time they are collaborating for a Coloring Lessons X Your Love party. Their debut album Sugar Honey Iced Tea! was just released on BBE, providing deep insights into the African-American world with authenticity, emotion and rhythm – and should finally establish the duo as the legitimate successors to Masters At Work. Another guest DJ is Antal, co-founder of the Amsterdam record store and label institution Rush Hour.
