The album Disco Club is considered one of the most enigmatic masterpieces of electronic music. It was so far ahead of its time that when Rephlex reissued it in 2004, nobody could believe it had been recorded in 1977. Bernhard Fevre, who grew up in Paris, was inspired by the African clubs and rhythms of the city, and in creating his debut as Black Devil Disco Club, had only a small arsenal of electronic keyboards, a vocoder and looped conga drums at his disposal. The result was an epic journey into the depths of absurd electronic disco, marked by haunting vocals and twisted melodies. Since then, Fevre has released nine more albums in this style. For this weekend's Klubnight will be playing a live set at Säule. Also hailing from France is Berlin-based DJ/producer Perfo, who is making his Berghain debut. His releases on labels such as Mord, Illegal Alien and NRBK are defined by complex textures, tight beats and a ritualistic atmosphere.

Up-and-coming Austrian band Mietze Conte play fast-paced hyperpop infused with surreally sweet melodies that have found a home on Sumoclic, a Brisbane-based label co-founded by Maxwell Byrne (1tbsp, Golden Vessel) and Connor Grant (Akurei). For the Live From Earth Klub party, they will be playing live at Panorama Bar. DJing will be Mura Masa, a British multi-instrumentalist and DJ who has been producing electronic music since the age of 15 who found fame eight years ago with his track “Love$ick” featuring ASAP Rocky. As co-founder of the queer party series and collective MARICAS, ISAbella from Spain has made her mark on Barcelona's club scene, known for her furiously energetic, 90s-inspired dance sound. Berghain welcomes the maximalist hardcore sound of Ascent Vierge and Gabber Eleganza as well as DJ Gigola.

Optimo is the Scottish DJ duo of JD Twitch and JG Wilkes who, with over 30 years of DJing and running labels and parties, have contributed more to the club culture than can be expressed in such a small paragraph. From 1997 to 2010, they were behind the club night Optimo Espacio, which took place every Sunday at Glasgow's Sub Club and is considered one of the most influential party series in the history of club music. Optimo can be heard every month on NTS Live, and the label Optimo Music, run by JD Twitch, is about to release its 150th album since its founding in 2009. Six years since their last time joining us, they are returning to Panorama Bar for this edition of Klubnacht. This will be the Berghain premiere for Red Rooms, a Berlin DJ/producer who has attracted attention in recent years with his hypnotic, minimalist techno sound heard on labels such as Drawner and Rotterdam's Planet Rhythm, the latter of which released his album Emerge in April.

An Italo disco icon is coming to Panorama Bar in the shape of Fred Ventura, who helped shape the genre in the 80s with his extremely heartfelt tracks, equally influenced by “Blue Monday” and the hi-NRG sound of Bobby Orlando – and was later rediscovered, covered and featured as a guest singer by the music archaeologists from Clone and the entire Rotterdam neo-Italo crew surrounding I-f and Alden Tyrell. Six years ago, Alessandro Adriani, who's DJing tonight, released Ventura's cassette recordings on his label Mannequin, Future Unknown – The Lost House Trax (1988–1992) , documenting his enthusiasm for Chicago house, Detroit techno and early Warp releases. Italo aficionados can also look forward to Glasgow's hi-NRG DJ Bonzai Bonner, co-promoter of the LGBTQIA+ party Shoot Your Shot, and David Vunk, owner of Moustache Records and producer of glorious Italo house tracks such as “Lord of the Cockrings”.

Seoul-born, Washington, D.C.-based Matthew Cha is an electro-acoustic musician who combines a background in jazz with a passion for techno and an appreciation of post-internet aesthetics. His style, marked by saxophone and modular synthesizers, explores improvisation, minimalism and the power of the straight kick drum – as can be heard on his album A. I. Love U , released in April. His DJ sets explore the more experimental side of techno, often prepared on his Buchla synthesizer, combining its dynamic beats and percussion with whose futuristic sounds. Cha also runs the label Eklektik Elektrik and is a proven vintage soundsystem specialist, and we are delighted to have him with us for the first time at this edition of our Klubnacht, joined by peers such as Neapolitan techno pioneer Gaetano Parisio and Japanese master of hypnotic techno, DJ Nobu. Amsterdam duo Dam Swindle, who run Heist Recordings, will be DJing at Panorama Bar, bringing their skillful mix of classic house, obscure disco, African funk and soulful techno.

Who doesn't know “Bommsen Böff”, “Fittichklopfer“ and ”Venq Tolep” or any of the other bonkers titles that Robag Wruhme has come up with, which the world-famous DJ from Jena has been giving his equally bonkers tracks for more than 20 years? Four years ago, after more than two decades of DJing and producing, Gegen and Golosa regular Sherø fulfilled a dream by founding KlubKid Records, a label for queer artists; as a DJ, she plays a 90s house- and rave-inspired sound. London-based Francesca Macri aka System Olympia is influenced by erotic literature, late-night car rides and the romance of the dancefloor. Her catchy synth-pop is marked by lush pads, dreamy melodies and sex-positive lyrics, and she presents a monthly radio show on NTS. Tonight's line-up is rounded off Victor, the boss of local label Magic Power.

Los Angeles-based producer Greg Broussard, aka The Egyptian Lover, is a pioneer of the early electro scene. With his razor-sharp 808 beats, melodic hooks and vocoder vocals, his tracks such as “Egypt, Egypt” from the mid-80s were the West Coast equivalent of the Miami bass and New York electronic funk of the time and are rightly considered part of the b-boy canon. To this day, he releases fresh boogie jams on his label Egyptian Empire and collaborates with acts such as The Exaltics and Moodymann. He is playing live at Säule for this weekend's Klubnacht. DJing on the main floor, Nørbak from Portugal has made a name for himself over the last six years by releasing effective, big-room-compatible techno tracks on the likes of Jeff Mills' Axis, Token, Mord and the label he co-runs, Hayes. Hailing from Helsinki is the Finnish-Bulgarian DJ/producer Katerina, who has put out wonderfully spacey Balearic house tracks on illustrious labels such as Rekids, Running Back, Cómeme, Tigersushi and her own imprint, Kotarak.

Hot, hotter, Hamam Nights … With a name that could also be a subcategory of a gay porn website, Danish DJ duo Cockwhore & Macho have been a fixture of Copenhagen's queer party scene for over ten years, offering a percussive mix of disco and dramatic house from 90s New York. With his queer party Butts at Groninger Club Oost, Fafi Abdel Nour is a key community builder in the Netherlands and an excellent house DJ as well. As a third guest, Hamam host Sedef Adasi has invited industrious Brit Josh Caffé, a DJ, producer, party organizer and singer who, on top of his collaborations with Honey Dijon and Paranoid London, has recently caught attention with his new-beat and electro-inspired EP Free World for Cormac's Polari label.

“I make pink touches to concrete music,” is what Pink Concrete says about his musical philosophy, which means that his techno comes with an extra dose of emotion. The Istanbul-born, Prague-based DJ has organized Pinkies and other various underground parties in the Czech capital, released his first EP I Hate Millennial Architecture two years ago, and founded his own label hereandthere in 2023. For this edition of Klubnacht, he will be DJing on the Berghain floor. Denny Voltage could also be described as someone who adds a queer touch to his sound: As a music-loving curator, the core of various LGBTQIA+-friendly Würzburg parties such as Prism, and founder of the techno collective Avoid the Subject, she is committed to upholding the idea of community and plays a lively, genre-spanning mix of house, garage, techno and trance in her sets. She can be heard tonight at Panorama Bar alongside a rare house set by Fadi Mohem.

La Noche is a queer party series by Byron Yeates and the S-candalo duo that focuses on community building, providing a safer space for FLINTA and the diverse musical interests of their artistic network. For the first edition of La Noche at Panorama Bar, on the eve of Christopher Street Day, they have invited Amsterdam-based Job Jobse, a DJ who is respected beyond the boundaries of individual dance subcultures, delivering emotionally charged, daring sets like no other – from house classics to emo trance, it's all-inclusive. Also at the decks: Body Language resident 131bpm and Londoner Angel D'lite, who serves up energetic, colorful sets that span house bangers, happy hardcore and trance.

Our Klubnacht for this year's CSD Berlin | Berlin Pride is with a packed lineup of musical and personal queer power. In addition to residents and regular guest DJs, special acts will be playing such as Auspex, a classically trained orchestral musician who has established himself as an uncompromising techno DJ in the Brooklyn warehouse scene, having organized the queer techno party ALTÆR in the New York club Basement for four years. From Los Angeles comes Colored Craig, a dancer, choreographer and passionate vinyl DJ, playing classic U.S. house in his sets with an absolutely infectious energy. Washington, D.C.-based Baronhawk Poitier is a DJ/producer, freestyle dancer and member of the queer artist collective The NeedleExchange, having released disco-ish deep-house records on labels like Honey Soundsystem and Nervous in recent years. Love is the message!

This edition of Finest Friday is uniting several generations of Berlin techno and house DJs at Panorama Bar. DJ Pete has been working at Hard Wax since 1991, making him the longest-serving employee of the legendary record store as well as a DJ with encyclopedic knowledge. In addition to his no-frills techno sets, he is also an excellent house DJ, as he regularly proves at the Power House parties at Kottbusser Tor and here with us tonight. Same school, new class: Berlin-based Yamour also worked at Hard Wax for four years, and she delivers a classic US-influenced techno and house sound, mostly playing vinyl. Tonight also features sets by Victor and Fadi Mohem, who filled the garden with jackin' house beats in July.

Hekt is the Danish multimedia artist Jesper Nørbæk, a member of Copenhagen's experimental music scene for more than ten years who's released several EPs as part of the duo Code Walk, most recently a trilogy on Peder Mannerfelt Produktion. As a solo artist, he has specialised in developing software for generative composition and synthesis in Max/MSP and has created systems for performances and installations in museums and galleries. His two dance EPs, Lens and Dream , released on Numbers, are distinguished by their brilliant sound design and refined songwriting, as can be heard in particular on the vocal tracks with Norwegian duo Smerz and American ballroom legend Kevin JZ Prodigy. Hekt will be playing live at Säule for the club night. Berghain premiere for William Fremaux alias Arkan, founder of Drawner Records and with releases on Setaoc Mass's SK_eleven, among others, who plays a deep, hypnotic techno style. Also taking part for the first time is Leipzig-based Lydia Eisenblätter, whose releases on Live At Robert Johnson, Shall Not Fade and her own label OAM show a powerful house sound inspired by classic US productions, sure to put you in a good mood.

This edition of Finest Friday kicks off the weekend with two live acts. Berlin producer Gilles Aiken, better known as Edward, has been releasing music for over 15 years that transports the improvisational nature and psychedelia of 70s cosmic music into the infinitely grooving dancefloor tracks of today. His albums on labels such as Giegling, Die Orakel and, most recently, Trelik oscillate between delicate, sample-heavy house and poetically meandering ambience. Steve O'Sullivan established himself as a master of subtle tech funk over 20 years ago. On his labels Mosaic and Bluetrain, he used influences from dub, reggae and Detroit techno to create slowly unfolding, minimalist techno tracks that have lost none of their pure and elegant drive, right up to his current releases on Sushitech Records.

Amsterdam-based Japanese DJ/producer Wata Igarashi has developed an unmistakable sound that takes the psychedelic character of the Roman school of techno to a new level with almost surgical precision. Igarashi was a skater punk and jazz aficionado before he began to develop his studio skills as a contract producer for TV commercials. His tracks on Bitta, Midgar and his own label WIP range from intense, prime-time energy flows to magical final moments on the dancefloor; his debut album Agartha , released on Kompakt, was his take on 70s krautrock and 90s electronica. DITA, who grew up in New Delhi and lives in Bali, began her DJ career ten years ago, mainly shuttling between Balinese and Jacarese dancefloors and developing a sound that spans proto house, disco and Balearic. She now has a residency at the new superclub Klymax, which opened in Bali earlier this year under the direction of DJ Harvey after five years of planning with customised audio technology and the finest wood. She will make her Panorama Bar debut at this edition of Klubnacht.

American DJ Jess Duran aka Succubass, who is deeply rooted in Seattle's LGBTQ+ music scene as a resident of the queer club Massive and member of the collectives TUF and High + Tight, describes herself as a “senior rave technician”. With her wide range of musical styles, she is a more than adequate booking at Panorama Bar for this edition of Darwin's Reef Party. One of the few women in the UK drum 'n' bass scene of the 90s, DJ Storm achieved cult status with her genre-defining sets, a legendary edition of the DJ Kicks mix CD series and her work for Goldie's Metalheadz label. Dubrunner is a DJ and producer from Leeds who releases monolithic half-steppers with dub techno chords, tight four-on-the-floor techno and elastic breakbeats on his Menace label. London-based American DJ/producer Oblig is another artist bridging the gap between the hip-hop subgenres of drill, grime and techno.

For several generations of DJs and music lovers from all over the world, the record store Hard Wax, founded in 1989 by Mark Ernestus, has been a taste-defining institution that has significantly shaped the Berlin techno sound with a rigorously curated selection and an early connection to the USA. DJ Jesse G, who grew up in Wiesbaden, has been working there for several years and has acquired a wealth of knowledge about electronic music during this time. The co-host of the radio show necton on Refugee Worldwide champions a metallic, trippy sound, which she is presenting at Berghain for the first time today. DJ Marcal, who grew up in the Brazilian city of Goiânia where he still lives today, takes on a groovy, raw and hypnotic approach to techno, which he underpins with widely played releases on Rekids, Enemy and Token. Memoirs of Hi-Tech Jazz was the name of one of Deetroit producer Waajeed's albums, and the term “hi-tech jazz”, coined by Underground Resistance, is also a good description for his productions, which cover a broad spectrum of African-American music, from jazz to hip hop (he once founded Slum Village with the late J Dilla), footwork, house and, of course, techno. ...

For this edition of her Jack Your Body party series, Honey Dijon has invited her long-time ally, label boss and co-producer of her music, Luke Solomon, who has been one of the UK's most active and skillful house DJs for over 30 years. Another guest is Fatima Koanda, a DJ who grew up in Bergamo and whose parents come from Burkina Faso and the Ivory Coast. The resident DJ of the queer party series Solida at Milan's DRK club, she plays a broad spectrum of techno, groovy house, electro and music with Afro-tribe influences, similar to Honey herself, and will be premiering at the Panorama Bar today. Club your body!

As a founding member of the experimental Leeds band Vessels, known for defying categorization, Lee Malcolm has a wealth of experience in breaking boundaries of genre. With his solo project Jinjé, he explores unconventional soundscapes that combine the acoustic with the electronic, the synthetic with the organic, demonstrating his talent for harmoniously uniting seemingly contradictory elements. After releases on Messrs. Kick & Drum and Ostgut Ton, his latest EP was released on Mesh, Max Cooper's label. Detailed layers of ambient dub meet trance maximalism, glittering pads meet house with R&B vocals, all with a surreal weirdo-pop catchiness. Jinjé will be playing live at Säule on Sunday for this edition of Klubnacht. Panorama Bar will feature Rob Manga, Tama Sumo, Lakuti and Soundstream, a quadruple threat that has already provided many lessons of love in this configuration at this venue. Kim Anh, an American DJ, producer and committed queer activist, whose 80s disco-inspired house tracks have been released on labels such as Live At Robert Johnson, Gigolo and her own Can U Not Talk, will also be appearing.

Liturgy is the project of Haela Ravenna Hunt-Hendrix, whose passionate and powerful transcendental black metal moves between metal, experimental and classical music. Their sixth album 93696, released last year on Thrill Jockey, is in four acts and the title refers to a numerical representation of the heavens. Their music, presented live at Berghain, collides with the deconstructed trance and hardcore sound of self-proclaimed rave voyeur Lorenzo Senni. The Berghain line-up at this Finest Friday x Light Metal Party will be complemented by a Sound Scenarioì by Iranian interdisciplinary artist Nazanin Noori and experimental producer Ziúr, who recently released an album on the Ugandan label Hakuna Kulala following releases on Planet Mu and PAN. The Panorama Bar will host DJ sets by Hamburg-based Haaizey, a selector of Afrobeats, Baile Funk and all things bass-laden, as well as Malaysian Wanton Witch. We'll end the night with pure bangers by the dutch producer and DJ De Schuurman's return to Panorama Bar.

“Change this Pain for Ectasy” is the name of Rex the Dog's latest single. With its driving chords, classic Moroder arpeggios and a voice pleading for redemption from all suffering, this disco anthem dedicated to nightlife, touching as it is effortlessly euphoric, is typical Rex The Dog hit material. The Brit with the cute dog avatar has been releasing music for 20 years, which he produces with a penchant for analog equipment and self-built modular synthesizers. His sound has changed and evolved over the years, but his talent for larger-than-life tracks and bold, fearless sound design remains. You can experience him live tonight at Säule for Klubnacht. Woof! Making her Berghain premiere is Marseille-born, Paris-based DJ/producer Hemka. Having grown up in a musical family, she first played bass guitar as a teenager before experimenting with two turntables and a mixer. Her techno sound is fast, groovy and slightly psychedelic.

Now based in Barcelona, Oriana from Montevideo, Uruguay, drew strong inspiration from the vibrant music scene in her hometown, where she gained attention for her buzzing sets that infuse house with spacey electro tracks, despite having little in the way of social media presence. Japanese DJ Masda, known for his extremely smooth mixing, has been active since the mid-90s, having put on underground dance parties in Tokyo for years before moving to Berlin. Through his label Cabaret Recordings, he has been pushing a deep, bass-heavy minimal house sound for 15 years, including the release of Evan Baggs' Not a Story EP nine years ago. All three are among the DJs playing tonight at PARAMIDA's Love on the Rocks party at Panorama Bar.

“Queer rejuvenation through techno” is the honorable guiding theme of the New York party series Merge, and as resident DJ, Clarisa Kimskii has been instrumental in the success of the raves that have been taking place in various Brooklyn venues for three years now. Her sets feature various subgenres of techno with long and sometimes aggressive mixing, revealing a creativity from over 20 years of DJ experience – to be heard at tonight's Klubnacht in Berghain. With his musical roots in 80s Miami bass and the city's b-boy culture, DJ/producer Danny Daze also shows a love for the underground electro sound of the Netherlands' Viewlexx crew. His releases on labels such as Schematic, Moustache and his own OMNIDISC range from experimental electro, electronica and peak-time techno to music for fashion shows; his debut album ::Blue:: , released last year, was a stunning soundtrack for an audiovisual planetarium show.

Seit neun Jahren verkaufen, vertreiben und produzieren Castro, Nemo und ihre Crew Platten in ihrem Laden Sound Metaphors in der Reichenberger Straße nahe dem Kotti. Mit viel Engagement und einem extrem breiten Spektrum an musikalischen Nischenstilen betreiben sie darüber hinaus sieben eigene Labels, auf denen man von Early 80s Disco, brasilianischer Musik, Italo Disco, Synth Pop, DJ Tool Whitelabels, Goa Trance Compilations und vieles mehr findet. Diese Vielfalt und Tiefe an musikalischem Wissen spiegelt sich auch im Line-Up ihrer Jubiläumsparty wider, zu der sie Freund*innen, Vorbilder und die erweiterte Familie eingeladen haben. Mit C3D-E & Hashman Deejay gibt es einen Live-Act aus dem Acting-Press-Umfeld (C3D-E ist neben dem ebenfalls spielenden PLO Man einer der Betreiber des Berliner Vinyl-only-Labels), die auf bisher zwei gemeinsamen Releases superben, soundstarken Dubtechno fahren. Ihr Live-Debüt in der Panorama Bar gibt die chinesische Künstlerin Xiaolin, deren ätherische Produktionen verträumte Electro-Basslines mit warmen, erdigen Percussions verbinden. Ebenfalls aus China kommt die DJ und Produzentin Temple Rat, die in ihren DJ-Sets schnelle Drumbeats, komplexes ...

“Good Boys Say Yes Sir” is the new single by Hard Ton, the Italian duo with the larger-than-life hi-NRG sound. With falsetto vocals, tongue-in-cheek humor and a contemporary interpretation of the gay New York club sound of the early 80s, they have been evoking a sensual disco dancefloor for 15 years, also taking a flamboyant bow (on acid!) to the great divas of their time, Sylvester and Divine. For this weekend's Klubnacht, Hard Ton will be performing live in the Säule. Over the last three years, Greek DJ/producer ANNĒ has made a name for herself releasing on Soma, Hardgroove and Mutual Rhythm with fast, heavy drums and a unique take on 90s techno, as can be heard on the Berghain floor. One level up is Neil McDonald aka Lord of the Isles, a DJ/producer who translates his love for the nature of the Highlands and islands of his Scottish homeland into wonderfully boundless music spanning ambient, deep techno, analog house, Italo and Balearic sounds. His latest album My Noise is Nothing , which he recorded with poet Eilen Renton, is a bold and evocative love letter to the great outdoors.

Born in Harlem and having grown up in Queens, Yvonne Turner is a pioneer of house music whose crucial contributions are often overlooked. In 1984, she released an instrumental dub version of Colonel Abrams' “Music Is the Answer”, which was played up and down in New York clubs The Loft and Paradise Garage and, with its stripped-down sound, paved the way from disco to a new genre yet to be fully defined. Turner continued to produce music into the 90s, including an official remix of Whitney Houston's “I'm Your Baby Tonight”, an influence on acts like Masters At Work and Kerrie Chandler. After working mainly as a teacher for almost two decades, she returned to music a few years ago, recording tracks with the late American disco vocalist Loleatta Holloway. Following her recent interview with Lakuti and Tama Sumo for a Resident Advisor podcast, we're delighted to hear the New Yorker at Panorama Bar for this edition of Your Love alongside DJ extraordinaire Maurice Fulton.

Hailing from Mexico City, Andy Martin is a Mexican-Jamaican DJ/producer whose music merges sci-fi techno with Latin American and Caribbean sounds. On his debut album Antigos Astronautas , released two years ago on his label Diaspora Echoes, he explores the history of a pre-Columbian civilization on the American continent said to have had highly advanced knowledge of astronomy, mathematics and architecture. Martin's approach to techno delves deep into rich sonic structures and mysterious atmospheres, subtly casting a spell on listeners. This edition of Klubnacht will be his first time playing at Berghain. After her debut at our Homodrop party in May, Berlin-based Brit Maze returns to Panorama Bar. Inspired by London's queer nightlife and kinky raves like Glastonbury's Block9, she has developed an energetic sound blending space disco and house over the last 20 years as both a DJ and producer (the latter as Maze DJ) on labels like Permanent Vacation and Neptune Discs. And there is a LIVE performance by Bolis Pupul in SÄULE tonite. On his recently released album Letter To Yu, the Chinese-Belgian artist deals with themes of loss, grief and origin as well as culture, ...

Thee Church Ov Acid House is the project of Jörn Elling Wuttke and Oliver Bradford. While Wuttke has helped write Frankfurt techno history as part of Alter Ego and Acid Jesus, Bradford made his debut just this year with a fantastic solo EP on nd_baumecker's Freund_innen label. Together they form a likable acid cult with two wonderfully eccentric releases on Pudel Produkte: hypnotic trance rhythms, ritualistic machine funk and rave piano bursting with euphoria. For their live set at Klubnacht on Sunday in Säule, they'll be conjuring up an Autumn of Love. US-American Josh Wink has also secured himself a chapter in the history of acid house with his 1995 TB-303 breakbeat mash-up opus “Higher State Of Consciousness” – since then, the long-term vegan has put out over 400 releases by more than 100 artists on his label Ovum, and he continues to excel at mixing techno and house in his sets – tonight on the Berghain floor.

Cybernetic Broadcasting System is a channel belonging to the iconic Dutch radio station Intergalactic FM, which has been playing a mix of Italo disco, disco, Detroit techno, Chicago house, old-school electro, ambient and exotica around the clock for the last 16 years. Ferenc van der Sluijs, better known as I-f, has been producing and releasing music for more than 25 years, maneuvering from grim acid techno to smash hits of Italo-inspired electro. On his labels Viewlexx and Murder Capital, he releases records by fellow acts such as Intergalactic Gary and Gesloten Circle with an uncommon underground ethos (the music is not available on streaming platforms). After his mind-blowing sets at Panorama Bar and the Lab over the past 19 years, we're looking forward to the first Cybernetic Broadcasting Systems Night with him and his gang – no station such dedication!

Hailing from Sweden, Alexander Berg first made a splash a decade and a half ago first as half the duo Genius of Time, then as a solo artist under the name Dorisburg, pushing a precise, hypnotic tech-house sound. Now that he and his label Aniara came out of a four-year hibernation, he is as alert and present as ever – after recording the free-flowing ambient album That Who Remembers with Sebastian Mullaert for Spazio Disponibile, he's now back to releasing his sprawling, cavernous dancefloor grooves with glittering melodies. Dorisburg will be playing live in Säule for this edition of Klubnacht. On the Berghain floor, also presenting his music live is Portugal's Nørbak – after releases on Axis, Hayes and Mord, he linked up with Token this year for a strong 4-track EP, striking the perfect balance between rawness and technical depth and finesse. You can also look forward to a DJ set by Californian techno producer Developer, back at Berghain after a five-year hiatus.

Honey Dijon, dance historian, fashion icon, collector of cultural artefacts, Grammy award winner and, of course, fabulous DJ in one, is playing an extended set at her Jack Your Body party in Panorama Bar, one week prior to the release of her DJ Kicks mix compilation. Enough time to express her transatlantic, epoch-spanning house sound in all its facets, transcending past, present and future. Warming up will be New York DJ, singer and producer Dee Diggs, who, like Honey, pays homage to the queer and Black origins of disco, ballroom, house and techno in her sets.

“I make noise and sometimes organize it in semi recognizable patterns”, says Moe Espinoza aka Drumcell about his music. He was born in the San Gabriel Valley of Los Angeles, a culturally diverse area that still influences his music today. In the early 00s, he and the Raíz brothers founded Droid Behavior, a group that gained notoriety for its Interface warehouse parties and the Droid Recordings label. Drumcell combines analogue and digital music aesthetics, developing a hypnotic, challenging techno style. Since the early 00s, he has been working regularly with David Flores aka Audio Injection, who also grew up in Los Angeles and started off DJing at underground warehouse and desert raves. In 2011, Flores launched his techno alias Truncate, giving his music a raw, minimalist funk that continues to be heard in the sets of techno giants to this day. For this weekend's Klubnacht, they will be joining forces once again on the Berghain floor after more than four years. For Soundstream's 25th anniversary he will also be joined by Romy Pope aka Snow made the rounds in Hamburg with practical training at Ladomat, a residency at Golden Pudel Club and a connection to Dial, meanwhile living in ...

Limousine Dream are back with their second party at Panorama Bar, with plenty of first-timers, a back-to-back set from label owner Gene On Earth and Dyed Soundorom, and a live set by Chicago house head Garrett David. The latter moved to the Windy City as a teen, where he became a buyer for legendary record store Gramaphone Records and resident DJ at Smartbar. His music, which he now mainly releases on his Global Swing label, is driving Chi-house with a European after-hours twist. Born in Caracas, DJ Mari.te has been living in Spain since 2018, where she runs Tresydos, her label and club night of the same name, and is known for her wide range of house-infused underground dance music. Under his alias Liquid Earth, Californian Berliner Taylor Freels crafts sun-drenched house tracks defined by deep bass and sharp melodies.

*Unfortunately the LIVE of Karenn cannot take place. Instead, there will now be a DJ set by Pariah. The House Gospel Choir is a London-based vocal group that first performed at the Glastonbury Festival in 2014. It was founded by singer Natalie Maddix with the aim of bringing together the musical styles of house and gospel – an approach pursued by many US producers and singers in New York in the 90s, whose roots in African-American churches paved the way for some of the biggest house hits of the decade. We're sure to hear a few of these songs during the performance (on Sunday, of course) by Harrsn with House Gospel Choir, leading a holy dance mass in the Säule with a slimmed-down club line-up and DJ. At Panorama Bar, Massimiliano Pagliara will be surrounded by a few musical mates, including Australian DJ/producer Eluize, who just released Curious Bliss , a summery, acid-soaked house EP on his label Funnuvojere. Along with Chris Cruse and Luigi Di Venere, he'll be playing a long closing set as Couple Looking For Bananas.

Laundrette is hosting another spin cycle, where David Elimelech, Partok and Roi Perez will be sharing the booth as usual with various queer DJs from all over the world. Samira is an excellent Palestinian house DJ, returning to Panorama Bar after the last edition of Laundrette. He was a resident at the Palestinian club Kabareet in Haifa, which sadly shut down in September after nine years. ISAbella is one of the co-founders of MARICAS, a FLINTA*-focused queer party in Barcelona that offers a vibrant mix of progressive house, breakbeats and trance. Kodemul from Mexico is mind behind Por Detroit, a queer party series in Mexico City and Los Angeles.

It's no overstatement to call him the voice of house music, instantly recognizable to anyone who's ever been remotely near a dancefloor: Robert Owens. His Chicago house-torch songs “Bring Down The Walls” and “Can You Feel It”, recorded with Larry Heard in the second half of the 80s, and “I'll Be Your Friend”, which he wrote with Satoshi Tomiie in 1991, made him a legend. Since then, Owens has lent his charismatic voice to countless records by acts such as Masters At Work, Photek and, most recently, Luke Slater and his project LB Dub Corp. Robert Owens will be playing at Panorama Bar for this edition of Klubnacht, and Slater will be playing live in the Säule with his house project. Berghain debut for Grace Dahl, DJ/producer and label owner (Serial Disc Touchers) with Finnish-Hungarian roots. As resident DJ of Vault Sessions, she's known for her versatile techno sets, propulsive grooves and powerful kick drums. Also playing here for the first time is Mandel, who has his own radio show called House of Highlife on Refuge Worldwide.

Time Passages is the label run by Germann Nguyen aka Binh, a DJ known for his devoted crate-digging and his extraordinary, comprehensive understanding of music. After growing up in Düsseldorf and moving to Berlin, Binh spent his formative years mainly at Club der Visionäre, organising his own party series over the course of years. Ten years ago, he released his Basic Trance Induction EP, bearing first catalog number of his own label – the starting signal for one of the most interesting house and techno imprints in Germany. Like many DJs and producers with roots in the minimal house scene, Binh works with a certain understated approach to media, releasing on his label exclusively on vinyl. To celebrate this tenth anniversary, there's a new double 12” as well as a tour, tonight taking him to Panorama Bar. Alongside Binh and Time Passages acts Nicolas Lutz and Latress, there will be sets from London Fabric resident Craig Richards and DJ Hell.

Secret Life of Machines is the title of the fabulous first album by Sterac, the Detroit techno alias of Amsterdam DJ/producer Steve Rachmad, which he released almost 30 years ago on the Dutch label 100% Pure. Back in 1968, long before AI, the writer Philip K. Dick was already exploring the philosophical question of whether androids dream of electric sheep. The answer remains unclear, but at least the concept of an empathic machine guided by its own feelings worked very well on Rachmad's debut. This certain mechanical funk, carried by an almost meditative melancholy, can still be found in his productions today. Sterac is playing on the Berghain for this edition of Klubnacht. The Paradox in Me , Terrence Fixmer's second album for Mute Records, was released this past summer, which he is now following with a live set of tight, EBM-influenced techno, also in Berghain. Finally, South Africa's Alan Abrahams aka Portable welcomes you to a Sunday concert in Säule with his profound love songs, edgy sci-fi broken beats and piano-heavy house anthems.

“What our tracks have in common is the vibe and the tempo. Almost all of them range from 122 to 130 bpm,” says Mathias Modica aka Kapote on his label Toy Tonics in a recent interview. “Here in Berlin, the techno tends to march, but with us, it swings. It's not a command to dance but rather a heartfelt suggestion.” That inviting approach also includes Dan Shake from Britain, who debuted ten years ago with his first maxi on Moodymann's Mahagony Music label. As a master at reinterpreting funk, disco and soul samples, he breathes new life into them with contemporary elements such as acid synths and heavy basslines. Venezuelan Gee Lane, who lives in Spain, released her first EP Metamorphosis on Toy Tonics in 2023. She has been DJing for over 20 years, works in a record store and was heavily influenced by the eclectic house sound of Body & Soul during her time in New York. Guinny is a new Toy Tonics act, a London-based DJ/producer, singer and multi-instrumentalist who fuses disco, house and techno. Meanwhile, Ruffillo will also feature Italo disco, Mediterranean and funky house.

Whenever Snax takes place, Klubnacht opens in two stages – Panorama Bar kicks off on Saturday night as usual, with Berghain joining in on Sunday at 16:00 for a shorter but no less intense session. Tunisian-Cambodian DJ/producer Azu Tiwaline brings her deep, ritualistic music, which sees techno as a natural evolution of the percussive music of her home terrain, the Sahara, while Spanish DJ/producer Kwartz, part of Oscar Mulero's PoleGroup, offers a raw, mental techno sound in his live set. At Panorama Bar, we can look forward to Chicago house legend Boo Williams, whose last set at the same venue was an overwhelming masterclass in uplifting house; Detroit's Loren, who recently made her debut with us on Pride weekend; and sets by Robert Johnson founder Ata and his Frankfurt resident DJ Franziska Berns.

DJ/producer Mala is a key figure in the British dubstep scene. Along with Coki, he founded the duo Digital Mystikz and the label DMZ 20 years ago, significantly influencing the early dubstep sound. Mala's output is characterized by deep basslines, minimalist rhythms and strong dub influences that create an almost meditative atmosphere – making it the perfect fit for Darwin's bass-heavy, eclectic Reef parties. Berlin-based producer Debmaster has been collaborating with Ugandan-born, Kenyan-based MC Yallah since 2018, with their joint release on Hakuna Kulala pairing the latter's wild vocals with the former's raw riddims. Kevin Martínez aka Niño Árbol is a Mexican DJ and the mind behind the party and label collective Ssensorial. DJ Earl, an integral part of Chicago's footwork and juke scene as a member of the Teklife crew, plays in the Panorama Bar. French DJ/producer Siu Mata has made a name for himself with bass-heavy dembow and innovating variations on dancehall, incorporating influences such as jungle, bass, tribal and trance rhythms into his music.

****Unfortunately the show with Rahim Redcar fka Christine and the Queens has been cancelled*** Rahim Redcar, formerly known as Christine and the Queens, is a prominent voice on the contemporary music scene whose work is characterized by a subtle fusion of pop, electro and R&B. Born and raised in Nantes, he connected early on with the queer art and drag scene, having a lasting impact on his artistic work. His international breakthrough came in 2014 with his debut album Chaleur Humaine . Redcar's music, which he describes as “freakpop”, is characterized by a constant examination of gender issues and his own identity. With his recently released album Hopecore , he's now taking to the dancefloors for the first time with passionate vocals, which can be experienced live at this edition of Klubnacht on early Sunday evening at Säule. Francisco Fernández Araya aka Zisko, one of the most popular younger techno DJs in Argentina, will be joining us again. During his set a year ago, he won new fans with his distinctive track selection, ending his bouncy set with a few disco tracks.

Tonight, Live From Earth Klub is bringing a small festival line-up of bands and DJs from different generations and musical parallel universes. The New York band Fcukers, led by the charismatic Shanny Wise and supported by DJ/producer Jackson Walker Lewis, has quickly made a name for itself in New York nightlife. Their music combines influences from 90s/00s house, trip-hop, big beat and indie rock, inspired by acts such as Daft Punk, Chemical Brothers and Todd Terry. In September, their sensational six-track EP BAGGY$$ was released on Ninja Tune sublabel Technicolour, and they're now playing at Panorama Bar alongside DJs such as Kulør founder Courtesy and Canadian electroclash pioneer Tiga. At Berghain, alongside Live From Earth acts such as Gigola, MCR-T and Theo B3K of Brutalismus 3000, the lineup includes none other than British rave cult band Underworld. For the very first time, they will perform together with B3K. Thirty years after their fantastic breakthrough album Dubnobasswithmyheadman, Karl Hyde and Rick Smith have just released their eleventh studio album, Strawberry Hotel, which is musically the best thing they've done in a long time. Even after more than three decades, ...

Decima is the debut album by Greek DJ/producer Stef Mendesitis, released in July, which is also the first artist album Ben Klock has released on his label Klockworks in twelve years. It's no surprise that Mendesitis should receive this honor, as his tracks – mostly recorded in one take – are among the best that can be during prime time at the moment: impactful, Detroit-oriented techno bombs, as Hard Wax so aptly described them. Tonight on the Berghain floor, Mendesitis will be playing a live set of his fast, dynamic music, driven by a certain Chicago funk. No introduction is needed for our other live act, Fadi Mohem, who will be performing his own material in the Panorama Bar after the WSNWG supergroup gig for this weekend's Klubnacht. Stella Zekri calls her mixing style “soulful playing”, which is characterized by a warm, physical energy and a love of vinyl culture. The co-organizer of the queer Berlin party series Body Language combines house, disco, dub and acid, and is also playing up at Panorama Bar.

Sedef Adasï is once again offering a deep musical massage in the steam room of her Hamam Nights series. Carlos Valdes has been a central figure in the Amsterdam club scene for more than two decades: as a DJ, label owner and organizer of the IsBurning nights, he has made significant contributions to the development of the city's electronic music scene and LGBTQ+ community. Fais Le Beau is the DJ alias of Luxembourg-born Diego Cozzi, founder of queer party series Gay Haze in Brussels. Musically, he is influenced by 90s house, trance and early 00s prog sounds. Hailing from London, DJs Peach and Shanti Celeste push an uplifting, swinging tech house sound. Peach released her first record on Celeste's label Peach Discs in 2021 before founding her own platform, Psychic Readings, two years ago. Both share a contemporary, British take on the classic Motor City sound.

Without Louie Vega, the history of house music would have to be rewritten. Hardly anyone has shaped the New York music scene over the past 35 years as much as this DJ/producer who grew up in the Bronx as the son of Puerto Rican immigrants. With solo projects and underground hits such as “Deep Inside” and “Love & Happiness”, Vega shaped the house sound of the Strictly Rhythm label in the early 90s with influences from salsa, Afrobeat, jazz, hip-hop, gospel and soul, which he continues to create today with an impressive array of guest musicians and singers. Together with Kenny “Dope” Gonzales, he forms the duo Masters At Work, which has produced countless remixes (the MAW playlist on Spotify contains over 33 hours of music) for stars such as Madonna and Michael Jackson, and released one of the best all-star dance albums of all time in 1997 with their side project Nuyorican Soul. Over the years, Vega has always been an excellent DJ, now bringing the original NYC house sound to Panorama Bar. The history of techno and electronic music in general would also be missing a few important chapters without the work of Moritz von Oswald. Alongside acts such as Chicago powerhouse maniac DJ ...

Ed Upton aka DMX Krew can look back on a 28-year career as a music producer, but his tracks still sound like the title of his second album for Aphex Twin's Rephlex label: Ffressshh! With an old-school approach that includes jamming with hardware, releasing up to three albums a year and a strict focus on the music, he playfully switches between electro, Italo, acid, house and synth-pop in a way that's pure joy. DMX Krew is playing live tonight at Panorama Bar for PARAMIDA's Love On The Rocks party. DJ TEETH and TPR, the founders of the queer Berlin party series Hypersoft, will be playing a mix of 90s progressive, trance, acid house and Belgian new beat.

Hailing from the UK, Mark Broom has been one of the most consistent techno producers of the last 30 years. Discogs lists over 180 12-inch releases and a handful of albums, and it can be assumed that he has had a hand in almost 1,000 releases. It all started in the Second Summer of Love in 1989, when he was a teen on vacation in Tenerife and got hooked on the emerging sounds of Chicago and acid house. Back home, he tried to track down these new styles, got himself a record player and laid the foundations for his musical career. For this edition of Klubnacht, Broom will play live on the Berghain floor. One of the first generation of Berlin house DJs is Clé, who was a resident at all the major house and techno clubs after reunification, such as Planet, E-Werk and WMF, still championing a super-sleek, Chicago-influenced sound today on labels such as Nu Groove and Rejected. He and his partner Eva Be will be playing at Panorama Bar. And the highlight on Sunday evening is a live act by Strahil Velchev aka KiNK at Säule – hyper epic!

We would like to give our heartfelt thanks to all the guests, staff and artists from the past 20 years who have made Berghain what it is today. Your diversity, energy, creativity and dedication have filled our house with life. None of this would have been possible without you - thank you for being part of our story!

Lakuti and Tama Sumo's last party of the year comes with a lineup that once again promises 100% Love & Happiness . Flo Real draws her inspiration from the R'n'B, soul, disco, funk and reggae of the 70s and 80s, influenced by her father's extensive record collection. Her musical journey began in the heyday of hip-hop, strongly inspired by radio shows such as Gilles Peterson's Worldwide. Flo Real delved deep into the world of electronic music, developing a versatile and soulful style characterized by jazz and afro-funk vibes. After working for Kruder & Dorfmeister's label G-Stone, she has been part of Moodymann's Mahogani Music crew and the Viennese house collective A Party Called JACK since 2010. We're also looking forward to the set by Kléo from Amsterdam.
Not so quiet or holy, this Finest X-Mas party at Panorama Bar focuses on local heroes Fadi Mohem, Katy De Jesus, Naomi and Rifts.

A trained violinist and member of the Oakland Youth Orchestra's first violin section, Ann-Marie Teasley aka AMX took up DJing five years ago and is now part of a new guard of Detroit DJs taking the classic Motor City techno sound into the future. On her EPs such as Sexworker , released on Deeptrax, she combines nocturnal, atmospheric electro beats, and her jams with Detroit legend Scan7 are raw machine soul. Having grown up in Bristol, vinyl-only DJ Jack Wallis aka Decka found his earliest musical influences in dubstep, punk and UK techno, which is reflected in his earliest releases on Darwin's SPE:C label. After moving to Berlin in 2017, he co-founded the Unterwegs label with The Lady Machine, releasing his own powerful and dynamic techno productions. AMX and Deka will be playing in Berghain for this edition of Klubnacht, while Panorama Bar will host a house set by DVS1 among other DJs.
